6463.desc:
A few captures of my A/DA MP-1 3 Tube Mod Rack, I ran every model for 200 epochs and the parameters for each model is as follows:

CleanSS - Solid State Clean Mode

Drive 1 > 4.0
Drive 2 (Compression) > 2.0
Master Gain > 7.0
Bass > 4
Mid > -4
Treble > 2
Presence > 2


OD1 - Tube Overdrive Mode

Drive 1 > 4.0
Drive 2 > 6.0
Master Gain > 8.0
Bass > 2
Mid > -4
Treble > 4
Presence > -2


OD2 - Tube Overdrive Mode

Drive 1 > 4.4
Drive 2 > 6.5
Master Gain > 7.5
Bass > 4
Mid > -4
Treble > 4
Presence > -4

MOD1 - 3TM Mod Mode

Drive 1 > 9.0
Drive 2 > 3.0
Master Gain > 9.0
Bass > -4
Mid > -2
Treble > 0
Presence > 0

A normal MP-1 Rack unit has 3 voicing options: Clean Solid State, Clean Tube and Overdrive while the 3 Tube Modded version replaces the Clean Tube voicing for another distortion voicing which is very high gain.

Hope you all enjoy!

45809.desc:
Ampeg SVT-CL – Bass Preamp Capture

Epochs: 1000 | Reamp Box: Countryman 85

⚠️Disclaimer: The online preview player does not accurately represent these captures. For the best tone and true-to-source results, please download the files and use them within the NAM plugin alongside a high-quality impulse response at the correct input gain.

Plug into the undisputed king of bass amplification with the Ampeg SVT-CL — captured to deliver the massive low end, punchy mids, and unmistakable tones that have powered countless legendary recordings and live rigs.

This profile delivers the classic SVT growl, depth and clarity: thick, weighty lows that stay tight, thick midrange punch for perfect mix placement, and a smooth yet present top end that preserves attack and articulation. Ideal for rock, metal, punk, and modern bass production where power and definition must coexist, mixed with some Sansamp and Darkglass for good measure!

Please Note: This is a preamp-only capture — no cabinet IR is included, giving you complete freedom to pair with your preferred bass cab IRs or power amp simulations.

Makes and Models
Ampeg SVT-CL

5494.desc:
Captures of various Analog Man SunFace fuzz pedals (standard architecture). I find that NAM has some trouble capturing these to the highest accuracy when they're at the fuzziest settings (C10), but they do get quite close.

Each AnalogMan SunFace pedal has a clean trimpot, so the C value is the Clean value. C0 is the cleanest while C10 is the fuzziest. When the clean knob is low, the fuzz pedal can clean up quite well. However, normal fuzz clean up with the volume knob won't work properly since the signal going into an audio interface is buffered, and fuzz cleanup requires a direct guitar signal into the pedal (since the cleanup has to do with impedance changes). At full volume on the guitar, it does get pretty close. You can be the judge!

66686.desc:
You'll find in this pack some settings of my Green Russian Big Muff Clone (flat mids) with the anti-aliasing treatment.

The (D)istortion has been set from 9 o'clock to 5 o'clock (Max), the (T)one from 10:30 to 1:30, and the (V)olume to about 3 o'clock.

For this set, I used a "super input" file and the REVzSTD architecture.

NAM files with calibration metadata included.

Technical information:
Interface: Motu M2
Reamp Box: Radial Pro-RMP
Reamp level: 12 dBu
Captures normalized to: -3 dB
Epochs per capture: 1000
ESR on REVzSTD mode: 0.0004219 to 0.001228

If you like my work, you can supporting me via paypal.
https://paypal.me/kosmicaudio

38560.desc: Capture of the Boss DS-1 Pedal with different Gain Settings (0-10). Volume was at maximum, and the tone pot was set to neutral

33515.desc:
[this is the Standard versions of the DS-1 captures. Check my profile for Lite (little bit less accurate, lower CPU) & Feather (even lower CPU)]

Here's lots of captures I made of a Boss DS-1, that I modified with the Wampler "Classic JCM-type Marshall Tones" specs.
If you want to know more about this mod, you can read this : https://www.premierguitar.com/diy/pedal-projects/boss-ds-1-mods
Pedal is Made in Taiwan, from 1993 according to the serial number.

This is my first time capturing real hardware gear and sharing it, I'm really happy with the results, I basically can't hear any difference between the real pedal and the NAM files.
My favorite profiles would be anything from Dist@4 to 8, Tone@4 or 6, going to a Clean Hiwatt DR NAM capture.

I made the captures at 4 different Tone settings (2 - 4 - 6 - 8), and 11 different Gain/Dist settings (from 0 to 10, 0 is minimum, 10 is max, 5 is noon).
Level was the same for all captures, around 7/10.

Recording chain :
V3 Sweep training file played from a looper pedal, going to a Boss DD-3 for splitting the signal (it has 2 outputs), 1st output to audio interface, 2nd output to the DS-1 and to the interface. I recorded both the dry and wet training files and used Dry/Wet Tone3000 capture, to be sure that everything was time aligned.
Interface is Universal Audio Volt 4.
Calibration metadatas provided in the files. (12.5 dBu)

5475.desc: This is a pack of 6 ds2 captures. Both "channels" 1 and 2 were captured. Included are settings of all knobs at noon, max distortion, and boost settings on both.

26513.desc:
Boss FZ-1w Waza Craft Fuzz
Tonex had a difficult time capturing the high gain settings, so trying some captures with NAM.  The highest fuzz captures are definitely improved in NAM vs Tonex.
Captures with Tone knob all the way up for the full, glorious fuzz effect!  Tame the highs in your NAM player of choice as needed.

Your Satisfaction tone is: FZ-1w Vintage l02 t05 f02 - just roll off the bass on your amp and/or guitar tone knob.

58569.desc:
I captured my Boss HM-2 Heavy Metal pedal. It is the MIJ version from 1985.

Settings approximated 0-10 in the following order:
Level - Color Mix L - Color Mix H - Distortion

The following settings were explored:
Level: 5, 7, 10
Color Mix L,H: 10,10; 9,9; 7,5; 5,8
Distortion: 0, 2, 5, 10

Three settings for the Level control are provided for those using input calibration to emulate the pedal's typical output settings.

The Color Mix L and H set very high at 9 starts to get the signature chainsaw tone.

I like running the Distortion at minimum into a distorted amp. However, higher settings are provided for those who want to run into clean amps. The difference between 2 and 10 isn't that drastic. The control is pretty much an off and on effect.

The following capture approximates settings that David Gilmour used on the album About Face
-Boss HM-2 Lv-7 L-7 H-5 D-5.nam
The Gilmourish and Kit Rae pages about David Gilmour’s HM-2 setting are here:
https://www.gilmourish.com/?page_id=52
https://www.kitrae.net/music/David_Gilmour_Tone_Building_1_2.html

Signal Chain:
RME UCX II Line Out - United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu
Boss HM-2 - RME UCX II Instrument In at 11.7dBu

I recommend setting the NAM Output mode to Raw or Calibrated if you use the auto input calibration feature. 

The captures were made with the TTSV10 input file, an experimental training file shared to the NAM community from The Tone Scientist (https://shop.thetonescientist.com/)

These were trained with the Standard Architecture at 1000 Epochs.

2709.desc:
The Hyper Metal distortion pedal was produced by Boss from 1993 to 1998, so it came after the Heavy Metal (HM-2) with a more brutal sound to it. Compared to the Metal Zone (MT-2), one could say it's more "raw" sounding, so to speak. 
This pack has 7 profiles, 3 of them from normal and 4 from boosted captures. 
I suggest trying NAM's eq for using this as a pedal or as a preamp by itself. For instance, reducing the highs to 2-3 could be more useful as a pedal in front of an amp, while highs at 7-8 could make it work right before a cab/IR.
I hope you have fun using this! 

You can support my work by donating here:
https://www.paypal.com/donate/?business=QY7JT5DWJB7NA&no_recurring=1&currency_code=BRL

41002.desc:
NAM captures of my Boss Bass Overdrive ODB-3.
All trademarks belong to their respective owners.
These captures are not affiliated with or endorsed by the amplifier manufacturer.

Reamp Return Level: 13.0 dBu (except Boss ODB-3 L14 L17 H17 B17 G17 -> 18.8 dBu)
Reamp Send Level: UCX 18.8 dBu | UFXII 19dBu

(All of my captures contain one of this values -> just enter the value of your interface in the NAM plugin settings + button "Calibrate Input" and NAM will take care for it)

Input Audio: TTS input v4.wav
Architecture: Custom/xStd

Reamp Signal Path
RME Fireface UCX or UFXII with Level = Hi Gain
-> Lehle P-Split III
-> Stomp / Amp
-> Fractal X-Load UK-Mode
-> RME Fireface

6142.desc:
This is the pedal the "old fella" at church kept pointing me towords when I was asking about tone. After seeing it on more then one board I thought I'd make a model of it. This is a dual overdrive, so two different cuircuts that are desiged   to be stacked with pleasing results. Enjoy! 

The Green side cascades into the Blue side when both drives are on, so opening two instances of NAM can replicate this. 


Technical stuff:

After some testing I found that there are only 2 non-linear knobs, Hence, they have been modeled each at 6 different steps. (12 models in total—6 of the blue side, and 6 of the green side. 

they were recorded to peak at -6dB, then gain matched using LUFS (integated) before running models all at least sub 0.003 ESR (most alot better)!

THE TONE KNOBS: 
These are esentially tilt eq/ super broad high selfs (they don't affect anything below 200hz). To achive the some effect place a high self at `~5800hz with an extreamly broad width so a 12db boost will look fairly linear untill around 500hz, while ignoring everything below 200hz.  IMPORTANT: the Tone knob is at 12 oclock (in the models), hence, between -12dB and +12dB on your EQ you can achihve the full range of the tone knobs. 

REAPER REAEQ:  freq: 5850Hz bandwidth: 4.00
LOGIC stock EQ:  freq: 5850Hz  Q factor:    0.10

EXTRA FOR THOSE FAMILIA WITH THE PEDAL:
if you would like to fully replicate the work flow of the pedal—simply open the following plugins (in this order): 

NAM (GREEN SIDE) — EQ (with high shelf) — NAM (BLUE side) — EQ (with high shef)

Gain and Drive knobs: Different Models
Level knobs: output gain in NAM
Tone knobs: High shelf gain ranging from -12 to +12 dB

Hope you enjoy, 

Cheers.

29380.desc:
Various captures of the Bugera 1960 Infinity modded (bright cap and negative feedback bypassed). In my opinion, it’s much better. The captures will be updated daily.
Please refer to the photo for the input numbers.

5876.desc:
The original Klon Centaur is known for its transparent quality and ability to add gain without coloring the tone too much.
As a faithful reproduction of this pedal, these qualities can also be found in the Ceriatone Centura.
Now, the Klon Centaur is a low gain overdrive pedal so don't expect high levels of gain.

For these captures, the Gain has been set from 7 o'clock (Min) to 5 o'clock (Max), the Treble to 12 o'clock and 1:30 o'clock, and the Output to 12 o'clock.

Technical information:
Interface: Motu M2
Reamp Box: Radial Pro-RMP
Reamp level: 12 dBu
Captures normalized to: -3 dB
Epochs per capture: 600
ESR on Standard mode: 0.0000047 to 0.00031

If you like my work, you can supporting me via paypal.
https://paypal.me/kosmicaudio

6165.desc:
If you like my work & want to support it, you can do so here:

https://ko-fi.com/2dornam 🙏

Ceriatone King Kong 100W tube head into a St. Rock React:IR II reactive load.

Some are EQ-matched to the frequency response (speaker impedance) of the amp plugged into my Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight cabinet.

Direct (IR required) profiles covering "clean to mean & in between" tones.

Most overdrive / hi-gain profiles within this set are shot with the Legandary Tones Mr. Scary mod in v2 of the hi-gain channel. Compared to choosing the clipping diodes, it adds a more natural (less harsh) sound to the signal.

Reamp chain details:

• RME Fireface UCX II -> SignalArt reamp box -> Amp -> Loadbox -> RME Fireface UCX II

Use the new "Calibration" feature in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

Additional details:
-----------------------

A few clarifications on the naming convention:

• [ReactEQ] = direct profile (IR required) but with EQ to match Mesa cab response
• [ReactIR2] = direct profile (IR required) with raw response
• Jose = clipping diodes engaged; no Mr. Scary mod involved 
• STD = uses the STANDARD architecture; works on the Dimehead NAM Player
• xSTD = uses a custom architecture (Андрей Полевой) from the NAM FB group

These are direct profiles so feel free to experiment with IRs of your choice.

56493.desc:
Ceriatone OTS Mini 20

A delightful boutique Dumble type amp.

Recorded on both the clean and the lead channels with EQ/Presence/OD Trim all at noon.

Notes:
ROCK / JAZZ adjusts the overall frequency response and voicing of the amplifier. ROCK has a fatter midrange and a deeper, spongier bottom end. You might find ROCK more familiar territory. JAZZ tightens up the bottom end and adjusts the contour of the midrange, giving the amplifier more of a hi-fi response

BRIGHT is a high-frequency boost that can be used to add sparkle to your tone in both clean and overdrive modes. This high frequency boost is more prominent as VOLUME is turned down.

MID BOOST is a midrange-frequency boost. You can use this to add fatness to your clean or overdrive tones, or even make single coil guitars sound thicker and less scooped. We particularly like MID BOOST with the overdrive mode.

PREAMP BOOST is noted to boost the output of the preamp stage and remove tone controls from the circuit.

OD TRIM control. This control adjusts the signal strength sent to the first tube stable in the OVERDRIVE channel. Lower settings yield more touch-sensitive, lower gain sounds. Higher settings are more compressed, higher gain, and more harmonically complex

Recorded using a Two-Notes Captor X in dual mono to avoid adding an IR to the profile.

Captured using a Motu M4 into Reaper, standard NAM profile at 800 epochs.

68062.desc:
The legendary Diezel VH4, one of the most revered high gain amplifiers ever built. Known for its surgical precision, tight low end, and articulate gain structure across all four channels, the VH4 is the amp behind some of the most iconic tones in modern metal and progressive rock.

The captures were made using a Two Notes Captor X+ load box paired with a Diezel front loaded 4x12 V30 cabinet IR, high grade cables throughout, and a meticulous capture process to ensure maximum fidelity.

All profiles are trained using the standard NAM file format. The pack covers  a wide range of gain settings and settings-

This is a sample pack showcasing a selection from the full collection. The complete pack includes 58 DI profiles, 57 full rig profiles, and 20 mix ready IRs, and is available at komposition101.com.

39647.desc:
"Featuring high and low EQ controls, a massive amount of gain and a die-hard chassis, the DigiTech Grunge Distortion summons swampy, searing tone with a quick stomp. Built to create the sound of from the muddy banks of the Wishkah, this pedal provides pounding tone and vicious attack." 
 It will tear your face off :D
 Thanks Robert :)
 Our setup:
(pedal, cables: Klotz - AC110, interface: NI KOMPLETE AUDIO 1, ART passive Dibox)
I think the "loud" output volume pot was set according to the circumstances. The recording level was around -10 db, normalized to -5 db. We paid close attention to the input level. We used an art di box wired in reverse. We tested it with mikko2, we didn't hear a difference lol.
If you have any comments, please let me know in the comments.
I hope you enjoy it. Of course we used the pedal AMP output.
 May metal be with you, peace.

If you like it, you can support it :) I don't ask, but I'm happy about it :)

https://www.patreon.com/posts/digitech-grunge-142731068?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link

35206.desc:
These captures are of my DOD 250 2013-era reissue.

Steinberg UR824 Line Out - DIYRE Line2Amp at 11.5dBu
DOD 250 - Steinberg UR824 In at 11.5dBu

My input and output were set at 11.5dBu so I could interface with my amp captures or Helix Native without adjustment.

I recommend setting the NAM Output mode to Raw or Calibrated if you use the auto input calibration feature.

Settings approximated in clock position in the following order:
(G)ain (L)evel

I evaluated settings using my Vox AC15C1 normal channel captures set a bit clean.

The captures were made with the TTSV10 input file as a recent experiment with Wet-Dry training. The TTSV10 input is an experimental training file shared to the NAM community from The Tone Scientist (https://shop.thetonescientist.com/)

These were trained with the Standard Architecture at 1000 Epochs.


4/5/26 - The following captures were added:
DOD 250 G-3 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-4 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-5 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-6 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-7 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-8 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-10 L-10 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-8 L-8 TTSV10
DOD 250 G-10 L-8 TTSV10

Slightly different return level than the first batch (by +0.2dB), but should not be noticeable.

New Hardware Chain used:
RME UCX II Line Out - United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu
DOD 250 - RME UCX II Instrument In at 11.7dBu

30435.desc:
This set features 66 unique captures of my Dumble ODS #102 Robben Ford clone. I used a Kauffmann Strat with slightly overwound vintage SSS pickups, a Maybach Tele with vintage Amber pickups and a Gibson SG with slightly overwound PAF Duanebucker Plus Pickups by Zhangbucker to dial in the tones.

This amp has three switches, namely rock, mids and bright. While the latter two are self-explanatory, the rock switch needs further explanation: It basically adjusts the tonestack of the amp and leads to higher gain as well as more top-end. The opposite position of Rock is Jazz, which has quite a bit less of the two attributes I listed before.

It has two inputs as well, FET and normal. For this set, I stayed with the normal input in order to preserve the tube tone as much as possible. 

The "Ford" designation is mainly used for settings where only the "Rock" mode is engaged and the "Carlton" designation is mainly used for settings where both the "Rock" and "Mids" switches are engaged. "All Switches" referes to settings where all three switches are engaged. 

This set covers a lot of nuances, it's best to pick out the captures that work with your own guitars and leave the rest be. Some of these captures have too much low end for a humbucker, others are way too bright for a Strat etc. and the nuances between the different captures can be rather small, i.e. the bass knob is turned down a couple steps or the input gain is at 4.5 instead of 3 etc.

There are three types of captures in this set:

- Standard denoted with an "S" suffix
- xStandard denoted with an "XS" suffix (more accurate, similar CPU hit as Standard)
- Complex denoted with a "C" suffix (highest accuracy, highest CPU hit)

If your computer can handle it, go for the complex captures, otherwise xSTD is a reasonable alternative to the standard architecture.

Very high quality 1400 epochs complex captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the TTS input V10 Test Signal and the hyper accuracy training architecture. Standard and xStandard captures were trained on tonezone3000.com with 1000 epochs.

All the output clips of this set were generated with the TTSv10 test signal, which results in much lower aliasing.

Signal Chain: UA Apollo X8 Line Output -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> Dumble ODS #102 Ford Clone -> Suhr Reactive Load -> UA Apollo X8 Hi-Z Input

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu (Calibration Metadata Included)

This is a pack of my ENGL fireball boosted and unboosted.
This amp have a huge amount of gain, so when i say boosted, it's just a sort of clean eq with differents pedals

30522.desc:
This set features captures of my ENGL Ironball 20W EL84 non-SE version. There are two different gain levels, rhythm and solo, that I captured for each EQ setting. 

Neither the clean channel nor the lead boost function were used for this capture set. The amp was running on full power (internal power soak wasn't active at all) into my Suhr Reactive Load.

There are three types of captures in this set:

- Standard denoted with an "S" suffix
- xStandard denoted with an "XS" suffix (more accurate, similar CPU hit as Standard)
- Complex denoted with a "C" suffix (highest accuracy, highest CPU hit)

If your computer can handle it, go for the complex captures, otherwise xSTD is a reasonable alternative to the standard architecture.

Very high quality 1400 epochs complex captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the TTS input V10 Test Signal and the hyper accuracy training architecture. Standard and xStandard captures were trained on tonezone3000.com with 1000 epochs.

All the output clips of this set were generated with the TTSv10 test signal, which results in much lower aliasing.

Signal Chain: UA Apollo X8 Line Output -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> ENGL Ironball -> Suhr Reactive Load -> UA Apollo X8 Hi-Z Input

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu (Calibration Metadata Included)

29603.desc:

54693.desc: Captures of my ENGL Powerball II connected to a Suhr Reactive Loadbox. These are hi gain captures of the Crunch, Lead 3, and Lead 4 channels. Some captures contain the Original Effects Halcyon Green overdrive in the "Mod" setting

41530.desc:
A capture for the EVH 5150III S 100W (EL34).


Volume knob was at noon for all capture, EVH's volumen seems to be more controlled.
Gain At 6 for Blue and Red.


Ch Green: every knob at noon, gain at 1 to capture the most clean of it.

Ch Blue: every knob at noon.

Ch Red: every knob at noon


No Boost Pedal.
No Cab.
No Pre or Post EQs.

6107.desc:
Nam captures of my 1971 Fender Bassman. I captured the normal channel with many different vol and eq settings with and without the bright switch. I also made captures of the common trick of removing the V1 preamp tube and changing the V4 PI tube for more vib channel gain. Included also are captures with Fulltone Fulldrive 2 boost, MXR EQ pedal, and Fulltone '69 Fuzz.

These are amp-only captures, but I have IRs of my 2x10 cab that I think work well with this amp: https://tonehunt.org/DahmanMusic/3209c177-8b49-4c04-a865-c0c151016503. My favorite way to mic this cab is with an R121 on cap of the bottom speaker, and an SM57 on the cone of the right speaker.

I hope you like it!

Captured using UA OX @24/48 through RME Fireface Converters at 600 epochs.
August 2024

6253.desc:
Fender Deluxe reverb 65 1965
Focusrite préamp
put a good oxford ir after and you will be happy!!
such a joy sharing my amps captures!

The Fender '65 Deluxe Reverb is highly regarded for its excellent sound quality and versatility.
It features a 12-inch speaker and 22-watt output, providing extra headroom and clarity, making it suitable for both low and high volume settings. 

The valve-powered reverb is noted for being noise-free with a wide range, from soft ambience to full-on surf sounds. 

Users appreciate its performance as a bedroom amp, retaining top-end detail even at lower volumes. 
1

Overall, the Fender '65 Deluxe Reverb is praised for its rich sound and dynamic range, making it a favorite among guitarists.

Fender ’65 Princeton Reverb Reissue for that classic Fender clean and crunch tones with great responsiveness. 2 × 6V6 power tubes, 3 × 12AX7 preamp tubes, 1 × 12AT7 and a 5AR4 rectifier, driving a 10″ Jensen C10R speaker ~12 watts.

Captured with both a Shure sm57 and a Beyerdynamic M160 as well as sum of both into API 1608 mic pres.

Volume at 3, 5 and 7 for clean, edge of breakup and crunch.

Recorded at Dimension 70 in NYC

68651.desc:
Here are captures of my Fender Super Reverb’s Normal Channel. A nice surprise was that TONE3000 was able to train these captures to the new A2 architecture. Props to Steve for pushing NAM forward and for TONE3000 to retrain such a large existing library.

See the Linked Tones for my captures of the Vibrato Channel. The Normal Channel sounds similar to the Vibrato Channel with the Mids about 8.

The Normal Channel is limited to the following controls:
(V)volume (T)reble (B)ass

The overall gain of the Normal Channel is perhaps less than the Vibrato channel.

Volumes 4-6: Clean to breakup
Volume 7: Light crunch
Volumes 8-10: Classic rock crunch

Some possible starting points:
Clean: BFSR Nrm V-4 T-4 B-4 SRL 6dB.nam
Breakup: BFSR Nrm V-7 T-6 B-3 SRL.nam
Crunch: BFSR Nrm V-8 T-8 B-2 SRL.nam
Maximum Gain: BFSR Nrm V-10 T-10 B-2 SRL.nam

I've provided enough captures for you to simulate turning up and down the Treble and Bass in the most useful ranges.

I turn NAM’s Output Volume up 12dB for captures of the amp’s Volume set at 4. The loudness of the captures with the Volume at 5 or 6 are much more to my expectations, with the loss of clean headroom. The captures were made immediately afterI finished the Vibrato Channel. These captures are an extension of those sessions, and the Linked Tones make up a complete interpretation of the amp from my end.

Signal Chain:
RME UCX II Line Out – United Studio Technology Replay Box at 11.5dBu -
Fender Super Reverb - Weber Z-Matcher -
Suhr Reactive Load -  RME UCX II Instrument In at 11.7dBu

Input/output calibration metadata for 11.5dBu send/11.7dBu return provided.

These captures were originally trained with the Standard input file at 1000 Epochs.

6/4/26 - I just uploaded the A1 captures of this amp for anyone who would rather want those captures.

As previously mentioned, TONE3000 retrained these to the new A2 Architecture.

Impulses of the amp's P10R speakers are also in the Linked Tones.

31452.desc:
If you like my work & want to support it, you can do so here: 

https://ko-fi.com/2dornam 🙏

The Salvation Audio UNNAMED is a boutique preamp recreation of the revered "Fortin Meshuggah" for the Egnater / Randall MTS / Synergy platforms.

A collection of quality, calibrated profiles (Standard architecture & xSTD architecture) at several of combinations to capture some of the range of tones this preamp is capable of. 

Use the new "Calibration" feature in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

Reamp chain details:
----------------------------

• RME Fireface UCX II -> SignalArt reamp -> Synergy SYN1 (with preamp) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 22.85 dBu
• RETURN level: 13.25 dBu

Additional details:
-----------------------

A few clarifications on the naming convention:

• Hi = the Input switch was flipped to the "High" position
• Lo = the Input switch was flipped to the "Low" position
• Up = the "Pull Gain" knob was in the UP position (no clipping diodes)
• Down = the "Pull Gain" knbo was in the DOWN position (clipping diodes)
• [PRE] = profile of just the preamp section. 
You need to load / use a poweramp sim/profile + IR/cab after the preamp profiles or route them into a real tube poweramp + cab

- Maxon OD808 (not for all captures)
- Friedman - BE-100
- Trace Elliot 4x12 Cab [8 Ohm] (for Load only)

HG = High Gain
MG = Mid Gain
LG = Low Gain
EOB = edge of Breakup
CLN = Clean

64322.desc:
Ladies & gentlemen - the Friedman BE100 Deluxe

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Friedman BE100 Deluxe NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ Summary

To celebrate my first commercial NAM pack release, I’m sharing a subset of quality, calibrated profiles (STANDARD architecture & xSTD architecture) to showcase the range of tones this amp is capable of. 

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ All 3 channels (Clean, BE, HBE) covered
✅ Real cab (Mesa 4x12 OS straight) used as load (no loadbox)
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD and xSTD architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack contains:

• 271 x AMP profiles (each captured in DI, SM57, SM58 and a SM58/SM57 blend)
• 150 x PRE profiles
• 54 x POW profiles (each captured in DI, SM57, SM58 and a SM58/SM57 blend)

… that’s 1,450 individual NAM files 😳

But all profiles are trained twice in both STD (default NAM Standard architecture) and xSTD (slightly enhanced NAM architecture for increased accuracy developed by Андрей Полевой) …

🤯 which takes it to 2,900 individual NAM files with the xSTD architecture accounted for.

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across all platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles each come in 4 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics blended (Shure SM57 & SM58)

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Friedman BE100 Deluxe -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight load) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu
• RETURN level: 13.195 dBu

Use the new "Calibration" feature in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp) of the amp head
• [PRE] = just the preamp section; use a poweramp + IR or tube power amp & cab with it
• [POW] = poweramp only profile; use a preamp with it (and IR if using the DI flavor)
• BE100DLX-CLN = the amp was using the CLEAN channel
• BE100DLX-BE = the amp was using the BE (Brown Eye) channel
• BE100DLX-HBE = the amp was using the HBE (Hairy Brown Eye) channel
• STD = uses the STANDARD architecture; works on the Dimehead NAM Player
• xSTD = uses a custom architecture (Андрей Полевой) from the NAM FB group


📒 Spreadsheet with settings & inspiration for all BE100DLX profiles:

https://mega.nz/file/L5ZV1aTK#gx_ZjwhdF3Hv00AP5gl_ehM3QlzaYIL0mDVcOt6GGYI

65779.desc:
DRPS Friedman BE-OD profiled with variations on all knobs. Naming convention as follows:

B - Bass T-Treble P-Presence G-Gain T-Tight

Volume set at 12 o clock for all profiles.

Enjoy the profiles, leave a comment if you like or dislike them. We would love to hear your opinion.

58979.desc:
The 🔥 Friedman “Jose Arredondo” 🔥 - Dave Friedman's tribute to the pioneer of hot-rodded British tones

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Friedman “Jose Arredondo” NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ “MultiLoad” - what’s this about?

The cabinet (or load) a tube amplifier interacts with has a considerable impact on the tone & feel. 
For this pack, I used 2 iconic cabinets and reamped all AMP and POW settings with each cab.  

ℹ️ Summary

The pack features a Friedman "Jose Arredondo" (EL34) amp captured using 2 iconic cabinets loading down the amplifier. 

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ 2 iconic guitar cabs used as loads (no reactive load):
🟣 Mesa 4x12 OS Straight
🟣 Marshall 4x12 1960BV with Marshall G12 Vintage speakers
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD and REVyHI architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack includes includes:

• 200 x AMP profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 44 x POW profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 70 x PRE profiles
• 40 x OD pedal profiles

TOTAL: 3,148 individual NAM files (1,574 in STD architecture and 1,574 in the REVyHI architecture)

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles offer 6 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND #1: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58) blended
• BLEND #2: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58 @ +4dB) blended
• BLEND #3: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 @ +4dB & SM58) blended

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Friedman "Jose Arredondo" -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight / Marshall 4x12 1960BV) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu

Use the "Calibration" feature (video linked) in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp)
• [PRE] = preamp only (no poweramp)
• [POW] = poweramp only (no preamp)
• [OD] = overdrive pedal
• N.PRES = Presence set to the “New” mode
• V.PRES = Presence set to the “Vintage” mode
• STD = standard quality architecture (A1)
• REVyHI = highest quality architecture (by Rickard Gerthsson)
• TSMINI = Tube Screamer Mini
• MXRM77 = MXR Custom Badass Modified O.D.
• FORTIN14 = Fortin Fourteen
• DWHAMER = PedalPCB Dwarven Hammer
• DFXKoD = DemonFX King of Drive
• DFXKLON = DemonFX Centaur
• DFXDBOOST = DemonFX Dual Boost, Exotic EP mode
• JR.TheJef = J.Rockett Audio Designs The Jeff Archer
• GRIND = Fortin Grind
• ODD = Warm Audio ODD
• BO$$ = Boss GE-7 Equalizer

📒 Spreadsheet with settings & inspiration for all profiles:

https://mega.nz/file/GtIQnTAA#vBLyGtxO5fqcWxLfnQ2smLA0u_fm4xW_wPREIa3-Vd0

6357.desc:
Just an update of the previous captures of the Friedman SS 100. I used the Lehle P-Split III as the reamp box for this batch of captures as opposed to the Walrus Audio Canvas Reamp.

Very high quality 1200 epochs standard and complex training captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the V3 Test Signal and the 0.9.0 trainer.

Complex captures have a "C" suffix whereas standard captures have an "S" suffix.

Signal Chain: Apollo X8 -> Lehel P-Split III -> Friedman SS 100 -> Suhr Reactive Load

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu

5640.desc:
NAM Captures of my Fulltone '69 Fuzz from 2000.

Captured @600 epochs through RME Fireface UCX II.

Includes what I think are the most useful tones from the pedal. For some of the captures I adjusted the internal gain pot to achieve different hard/brightness or smooth/thickness of tone.

This is a great pedal. It really does that awesome "saggy" fuzz thing I like so much in the early Hendrix recordings, especially like in "3rd Stone from the Sun."

In this pack, you'll find some settings of my OCD V1.5 captured on 12 volts (slightly more headroom and less compressed drive), in LP (Low Peak) and HP (High Peak) modes and with the anti-aliasing treatment.

As for my other anti-aliasing sets, I used a "super input" file and the xSTD architecture for the training.

For these captures, the Drive has been set from 9 o'clock to 3 o'clock, the Tone to 12 o'clock and 1:30 o'clock, and the Volume to 5 o'clock (Max).

NAM files with calibration Metadata included.

Technical information:
Interface: Motu M2
Reamp Box: Radial Pro-RMP
Reamp level: 12 dBu
Captures normalized to: -3 dB
Epochs per capture: 1000
ESR on xSTD mode: 0.0002248 to 0.001210

If you like my work, you can supporting me via paypal.
https://paypal.me/kosmicaudio

6233.desc:
Hiwatt Custom 100 DR 103 1974
Focusrite isa one préamp
500 epochs
Hiwatt (stylized in all caps) is a British company that manufactures amplifiers for electric guitars and electric basses. Founded in the mid-1960s by Dave Reeves, Hiwatt helped define the sound of rock music of the era, alongside fellow British brands Marshall and Vox.[1] Pink Floyd, The Who, and The Rolling Stones all prominently used Hiwatt amplifiers, which were popular for their "big punchy" tone, high headroom, and durability

53037.desc:
Ladies & gentlemen - the Hiwatt Super-Hi 50 (Fortin designed) 50W EL34-loaded

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Hiwatt Super-Hi 50 (Fortin designed) 50W NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ Summary

The pack features a current production Hiwatt Super-Hi 50 (Fortin designed) 50W EL34-loaded head captured using a Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight cabinet loading down the amplifier. 

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ Real cab (Mesa 4x12 OS straight) used as load (no loadbox)
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD and REVyHI architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack includes includes:

• 66 x AMP profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 21 x POW profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 40 x PRE profiles
• 35 x OD pedal profiles

TOTAL: 1,194 individual NAM files (597 in STD architecture and 597 in the REVyHI architecture)

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles offer 6 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND #1: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58) blended
• BLEND #2: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58 @ +3dB) blended
• BLEND #3: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 @ +3dB & SM58) blended

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Hiwatt Super-Hi 50 -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight load) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu

Use the new "Calibration" feature (video linked) in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp)
• [PRE] = preamp only (no poweramp)
• [POW] = poweramp only (no preamp)
• [OD] = overdrive pedal
• STD = standard quality architecture; works on the NAM Player
• REVyHI = highest quality architecture (by Rickard Gerthsson)
• TSMINI = Tube Screamer Mini
• MXRM77 = MXR Custom Badass Modified O.D.
• FORTIN14 = Fortin Fourteen
• DWHAMER = PedalPCB Dwarven Hammer
• DFXKoD = DemonFX King of Drive
• DFXKLON = DemonFX Centaur
• DFXDBOOST = DemonFX Dual Boost, Exotic EP mode
• JR.TheJef = J.Rockett Audio Designs The Jeff Archer
• GRIND = Fortin Grind
• ODD = Warm Audio ODD

📒 Spreadsheet with settings & inspiration for all profiles:

https://mega.nz/file/GhgVkLZC#p6mrIGfGbVS8hindbJmL0jrmFUq1yjt0konLkK3BeT8

29760.desc:
Hotone Heart Attack © — Mesa Rectifier Inspired Reamp Tone

Dive into the fierce and raw power of a Mesa Rectifier captured through the compact yet punchy Hotone Heart Attack solid-state amp. This unique reamp tone blends the aggressive crunch, tight low-end, and searing mids that define the legendary Rectifier sound, all distilled into a compact format perfect for versatile recording setups.

Ideal for shredders, riff monsters, and tone hunters alike — whether you crave tight rhythm chugs or soaring lead tones, this capture delivers the bite and clarity of a high-gain beast without the studio hassle.

Get ready to unleash the fire and fury with every note!

30104.desc:
Ibanez TS808 Reissue (2003) This green soul is no clone — it’s the real-deal 2003 reissue of the legendary TS808, hand-wired vibe straight from Ibanez’s vault of tone history. It carries that unmistakable midrange push, a silky-smooth clipping that doesn’t scream, but sings — like an old soul on fire. Through a clean amp or a cooking preamp like the Mesa VIN, the TS808 doesn't just add drive; it enhances character, tightens the lows, and gives your pick attack a halo. When reamped with high-grade cables and a Radial DI, this thing feels like you're reheating vintage gold — no fizz, no digital grain, just analog warmth and honest push. The tone knob is subtle but musical, and the gain blooms naturally, never biting unless you ask it to. This particular unit is from the 2003 Japanese reissue batch, with that slightly warmer bottom end and smoother clipping than most modern TS circuits. Paired with controlled output levels (ID4 interface, Level 3–8 tested), it reacts dynamically — from bluesy sparkle to molten overdrive. If you’re capturing its sound for reamping, treat it like a classic ribbon mic: feed it right, and it gives you back emotion in waveform.

Files Keyword : 
Level = Lvl 
Gain (OD) = G 
Tone = T 
Signal Strength =Q (Quiet) M (Medium) H (Hot) VH (Very Hot)

30015.desc:
These are Standard captures of my Jet City JCA22H head through a Suhr Reactive Load (45 total when done). I modded the amp with a Depth knob. This particular pack is for the Crunch Channel.

Settings are noted in the filename in the following order:
Crunch Bass Middle Treble Volume Presence Depth

Three gain settings, 5 TMB settings, and 3 Depth settings on each channel (Crunch, Overdrive) were captured. I dialed the amp with the Depth at ~4 (11:00) initially, but wanted to catalogue the effect of dialing it down for reference. 

The Channel Volume was kept low, but I may revisit at higher volumes in the future.

Input/output calibration is 11.5dBu and metadata was filled in.

Training was set at 1000 Epochs on TONE3000.
The reamp box was a DIYRE L2A.
The audio interface used was a Steinberg UR824.

26841.desc:
This capture set mainly contains captures of the JHS Andy Timmons signature distortion pedal in isolation with a couple settings that I saw Andy use. The 100W (switch in the middle) was used exclusively, drive was set to about 1 o'clock and EQ and Air where somewhere between 9:30 (dark) and 12 o'clock (bright). Andy's main setting is around 11 o'clock for both of these controls. 

I also included a couple of captures where I've put the JHS AT pedal in front of my Rivera M100. It's not a Mesa/Boogie Lonestar, but it's still an EL34 amp with a blackfaceish clean channel.

The nomenclature is structured as follows:

C = Complex Architecture (My usual Hyper Accuracy Training)

S = Standard Architecture (Tone3000 Default)

XS = xSTD Architecture (Andrey's Standard Architecture)

All the complex captures were trained with 1400 epochs, all the standard captures were trained with 1000 epochs on tone3000.

Signal Chain: UA Apollo X8 -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> JHS @ (it stops here for the pedal only captures) -> Rivera M100 -> Suhr Reactive Load

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu (Calibration Metadata Included)

5925.desc:
JHS Bonsai Pack (9 Tube Screamers)

These are "Standard" captures at 400 epochs and are calibrated to work at 13dBu.

All the info about the pack is in the linked video but at a glance the setup is:

Neutral - Drive 0 | Tone 5 | Vol 5
Boost - Drive 0 | Tone 5 | Vol - 10

57368.desc:
This pack contains 8 captures of the JHS Morning Glory v4, each with different settings. This is a rework of a previous pack I made, altered to fit better with the NAM plugins.

V = Volume
D = Drive
T = Tone

This time, I only used 2 positions for Volume (V5, V8) and 2 positions for Tone (T3 and T8), because both NAM plugins have an EQ and Input/Output controls. 

EDIT: After testing, I found that both volume options sounded identical, so I kept only V5 files. there is now 1 V position, 2 T positions and 4 D positions.

Trained on feather with 800 epochs 

Enjoy :)

27702.desc: JHS Kilt Overdrive in 2 different pedals: v1 and v2. Based on legendary ZZ Top Band guitarist, Billy Gibbons.

44987.desc:
My captures of my Joyo Bantamp Meteor II.

All have tone on 2 o'clock and volume around 10 o'clock with varying gain.

There may be mistakes in gain value approximations, because I couldn't be bothered to do it precisely lol.

Normalized to -6dB.

Did it most of all as a test, because I didn't want to have this amp turned on all the time and honestly full gain is where it's at.

Cheers!

5196.desc:
Several captures of an original silver Klon. A few of my favorite settings included, as well as a "John Mayer" setting that I found from a fan photo who had Mayer sign his Klon and mark his favorite setting on it.
Because a Klon is useful as a boost, I have found that if you turn it up on the OUTPUT side of things, it will push the front end of the next thing in the chain in a nice way...so run this on its own instance INTO an amp capture and crank it on the way out.

565.desc: A klon centaur diy pedal at multiple settings.

44519.desc:
Willy's KSR Ceres One Mini Preamp Pedal Collection

I used to own a KSR Ceres Pedal a few years ago. For the first few months of using the pedal, i did not achieve any notable results however, once i realized how well the preamp is voiced to cut through crushing drummers and big bass players in techy and modern metal: I heard the KSR sound. The Ceres is a fine example of how an amp designer like KSR solves problems for recording and gigging artists; you must try these captures in a mix or with a live band to hear how well the pedal has been voiced. These captures are a small sample of this recording tool.

This pack captures the KSR Ceres One, a preamp distilling the grind, girth and guts of coveted full-sized KSR amplifiers. These captures feature the specific "V2" layout (where Up=Thick, Down=Fat) with extra gain on tap compared to the original run. All the captures have Gain and EQ at noon.

🎛 Signal Chain
Captured with reference-class hardware, using a RME Babyface Pro FS and a United Studio Technologies Replay Box. We utilized the Replay's Harmonic setting at max gain—The Replay Box/Signal Art Reamp Boxes are not transparent; they add harmonics. 

Calibration
In: 4.95 dbu
Out: 11.27 dbu

📂 The Captures

Pristine cleans, ranging from warm (tone < noon) to bright (tone > noon).
Clean1 - Pedal Platform
Clean2 - Chimey

Dynamic, open rock tones covering Thick, Tight, and Fat responses.
Rhythm1 - Ares/Orthos Crunch - Thick
Rhythm2 - Ares/Orthos Crunch - Tight
Rhythm3 - Ares/Orthos Crunch - Fat

The aggressive modern metal standard. From djent-ready precision to a massive wall of sound.
Rhythm4 - Artemis/Gemini Red Mode - Thick
Rhythm5 - Artemis/Gemini Red Mode - Tight
Rhythm6 - Artemis/Gemini Red Mode - Fat

Saturated, liquid sustain for soaring leads across all three Feel positions. These tones excel at Riffing as much as they help solos cut in a dense mix.
Lead1 - Colossus Lead 1 - Thick
Lead2 - Colossus Lead 1 - Tight
Lead3 - Colossus Lead 1 - Fat

Perfect for fans of Jeff Loomis, Keith Merrow, and articulate heavy tones. This incredibly tight circuit loves down tuning and extended range instruments.

I highly recommend picking up this pedal to try out its range of tones, pairing it with a power amplifier or interesting post-pedal color boxes, Tube Drives, Exciters or other dynamics pedals into an IR to craft your chain.

Made with love by iconoclastaud.io

B.B.King Lab Series 5 High Input by TRD AXEFXIII based on red sun fx Lab Series L5 Preamp and created with Fractal AXE FX III.

Refer to link for all details.

45914.desc:
Capture of a Laney Ironheart IRT120 head (original pre-BCC version) with 6L6 power tubes. Captured using a Torpedo Captor 16.

I dialed in an open, slightly scooped tone on a lot of the models so they can take well to a SD-1 / TS style boost without getting overly honky / losing too much low end

Settings:
- "clean" clean B 5.5, M 4.5+, T 8, Dyna 6, Tone 5
- "crunchy" lead B 10+, M 2+, T 8, Dyna 3, Tone 4, Gain 6
- "growl" lead B 10, M 2+, T 8, Dyna 7, Tone 4, Gain 6
- "lead" lead B 4.5, M 5, T 5+, Dyna 3, Tone ?, Gain 6
- "modern" rhythm B 7, M 3.5, T 3, Dyna 5, Tone 10, Gain 7
- "doom" clean B 10+, M 10+, T 10+, Dyna 6, Tone 5, Vol 8, Boost 4

28736.desc:
This is the capture of my Laney Ironheart IRT60H as I play it in the rehearsal room with my band Teariscope. It contains Captures including and excluding the Power Amp. This was made by using a DI Box in the FX Loop of the AMP and an St.Rock React:IR Load Box.
This is the Amp only. To get the same tone as me, use a tight gate and:

 - Lead: EQ -> TubeScreamer -> Amp 
 - Rhythm: TubeScreamer -> Amp
 - Clean: Compressor -> Amp

With these Pedal seetings:
 - EQ Pedal: Reduce the low end (everything under 1kHz), boost the highs a tad, boost the whole signal slightly.
 - TubeScreamer: Level Full, Tone slightly beyond noon, no drive
 - Compressor: Reduce the peaks, push the signal.

Update 13.06.2025: Added Version of Boosted Amp using EHX East River Drive (cheap TubeScreamer copy) with aforementioned settings.

Update 13.03.2026: Re-Captured the Amp with new knowledge, did everything to circumvent tonesuck in the reamping chain. New models include my new naming schema. A "p" means that the pot was pulled to change the frequency range of the band.

Naming Schema
=============

Reamping Related
-------------------------
 PWR - PowerAmp
 PRE - PreAmp
 OD - OD Boosted

EQ (-5 to 5 | p = pulled pot)
------------------------------------
 B - Bass
 M - Mid
 T - Treble

Overall Settings
---------------------
 G - Gain (0 to 10)
 D - Dynamics (%)
 TN - Tone (-5 to 5)

45200.desc: This NAM pack is designed for purity, capturing the amp head's sound across multiple gain stages with a neutral EQ. For extra aggression and clarity, switch to the included Treble Boost captures (Treble set to 7). All profiles are trained with 1000 epochs for a premium, studio-grade experience. Just add your IR and you're ready to go.

59604.desc:
Purchase the original pedal, handmade in CZ, here:
https://lokajaudio.com/products/lokajaudio-der-blend

This fuzz pedal makes a literal digital toy sound like an expensive analog synth! A personal favorite for lush sounds. Probably sounds pretty good on guitar too lol, since it's a clone of the Fender Blender, famously used by famous artists such as Kevin Shields (My Bloody Valentine) and Billy Corgan.

NAM 0.12.2

Knobs are labeled 0-9, but the pots' range is 1-9.
"Boost" is a foot switch, so it's either 0% or 100%.
Example:
`9sus_9tone_5level_9blend_0boost` =
Sustain all the way up, Tone all the way up, Level slightly over 50%, Blend all the way up, Boost footswich off (0%).

Please note that all of the `0sus_0tone_Xlevel_0blend_0boost` NAM model/profile/capture (whatever people are calling a checkpoint these days) files are basically just reference points, and will probably not be very useful for things like "actually playing an instrument" or "making music"! If you just want to jam with some cool tones, use the files which have Sustain, Tone, Blend values above 0!

[C300] = COMPLEX, 300 Epochs, trained with a custom fork of NAM.
[RyH300] = REVyHi, 300 Epochs, trained with a custom fork of NAM.
[RyS300] = REVySTD, 300 Epochs, trained with a custom fork of NAM.
[RxS300] = REVxSTD, 300 Epochs, trained with a custom fork of NAM.
[S300] = STANDARD, 300 Epochs, trained with a custom fork of NAM.
[T3K] [S1000] = STANDARD, 1000 Epochs, trained on T3K, no secret special sauce.

Model architectures explained:
STANDARD = Baseline, "normal" NAM Architecture, should play nice with most hardware, about 290 kB.
REVxSTD = Basically the same requirements as Standard, but sometimes better quality, about 290 kB.
REVySTD = Similar to REVxSTD, but possibly better, a bit more demanding, slightly larger file size.
REVyHI = Close to COMPLEX in terms of accuracy, about 480 kB.
COMPLEX = The absolute highest NAM quality! You may need a more powerful computer, and some hardware will not be able to handle this. I recommend using a less demanding Architecture for tracking, and then using COMPLEX for offline rendering. Size is about 1900 kB.

Signal chain:
Ableton Live 12.3 Suite
ZOOM UAC-232 32-bit float 48 kHz 100% gain
ART Dual RDB 100% gain
Lokajaudio Der Blend
ZOOM UAC-232 32-bit float 48 kHz input level 12
Ableton Live 12.3 Suite

Converted to 24-bit integer 48 kHz mono and normalized to -3 dBFS TPL via dBpowerAmp.

ZOOM UAC-232 100% output, using a sine wave input at approximately 1.00 kHz measuring just barely under 0 dBFS TPL at the DAW output (double checked with YouLean Loudness Meter 2 Pro) measures 3.518 or 3.559 Volts RMS, which converts to 13.14 dBu or 13.245 dBu, respectively. I list 13 dBu. I used an AstroAI TRMS-6000 multimeter, and triple-checked the measurements. The ZOOM UAC-232 Hi-Z TRS input was set to +12 dBu, and the level measured in the DAW was just under -8 dBFS. I list 4 dBu.
Power was provided by a Mosky ISO-10 PSU, which has been proven to not have continuity between the individual output grounds, and which uses a high-impedance LVR to isolate the grounds.

Computer specs:
ROG Z13 ACRNM
i9 13900H
32 GB LPDDR5
Nvidia GTX 4070 Mobile
Windows 11 Pro

You can use my fork of NAM to make your own "Hyper Accuracy" aka "COMPLEX" architecture NAM profiles, as well as "REVxSTD", "REVyHI", and other Architectures:
https://github.com/sskalnik/neural-amp-modeler

Stagebug connects headphone out to interface input
Interface preamp set to "Pure", Z-tone set to 100% "Sharp"

File numbers represent settings for the amp's dials left to right.
Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, Volume I, Volume II

61531.desc:
These are captures of the Leadchannel of the Marshall 6100 - 30th anniversary head. 

This is the high gain channel. I made captures with and without boost pedals.

There will be a "gain" designation in the file names, this refers to the setting of the gain knob on the amp.

low-gain : about 10 o'clock
mid-gain: about 1 o'clock
high-gain: about 4 o'clock

The profiles with have "gb" in their name, have the gain boost switch engaged.

If you like and use my work, a cup of coffee is appreciated: https://ko-fi.com/perimeteraudio

52421.desc:
Marshall Valvestate 2000 AVT50H High gain sounds. Recorded from the preamp out (FX send). 

The amp has been modded by removing the reverb tank connector from the main pcb which turns the Reverb pot into a sort of presence control. However, this shouldn't affect the sound here, as it seems to effect the poweramp and not the preamp.

All nams in this pack have the Gain set on 10 and volume on 5 on the Overdrive channel.

53272.desc:
This is a set of EQ-neutral, head-only models captured from a Marshall DSL40CR using a passive Suhr Line Out Box. If you're looking for a full-rig capture of this amp with its factory speaker, try my Marshall DSL40CR EQ-Neutral Full-Rig models, which were captured at the same time using an SM57 microphone. Or, try this head-only set with the Celestion V-Type IR uploaded by @outmodedelectronics (which sounds very similar to the real amp with its factory speaker): 

https://www.tone3000.com/tones/celestion-v-type-line-6-spider-valve-4x12-28178

Treble/Middle/Bass on the amp were kept neutral at 5, giving you the final say in how the tone is ultimately shaped in the NAM player. Presence and Resonance were increased on the higher-gain/OD models to compensate for losses due to the added compression. Reverb was set to zero for all captures. Power mode was set to High (40W), and Master volume was kept constant at 3. 

No reactive load devices were used, and no manual processing was done to either the sweep signal input file nor the output audio files used to train these models. Like it or not, this is the raw sound that the DSL40CR produces :)

The models listed below are organized in-order of increasing gain level (from 1 to 10) and increasing gain stages (Clean/Crunch/OD1/OD2). This lets you explore the full range of gain on this amp, as adjacent models overlap each other to some degree. For example, Model 3 at higher guitar volume will sound similar to Model 4 at lower guitar volume. Choose a model that's closest to the amount of distortion/breakup you want, then adjust the EQ in the NAM player as you see fit. Hope you guys have fun with these.

Model Names - Knob Settings (descriptions):

 Classic Clean mode:
  1. Pristine Clean - Gain 1.5, Vol 8, Pres 4.5, Res 4.5 (super clean, max headroom, good pedal platform)
  2. Dynamic Clean - Gain 4, Vol 7.5, Pres 5, Res 4.5 (more bloom and dynamic response)
  3. Edge-Glow Clean - Gain 7.5, Vol 5.5, Pres 5, Res 4.5 (reaches edge-of-breakup with harder picking and increased guitar volume)
  4. Point Break Clean - Gain 10, Vol 5, Pres 5, Res 5 (crosses edge-of-breakup with harder picking and increased guitar volume)

 Classic Crunch mode:  
  5. Straightjacket Crunch - Gain 1.5, Vol 3, Pres 5, Res 5 (reaches edge-of-breakup with lighter picking and reduced guitar volume)
  6. Open Crunch - Gain 3.5, Vol 2, Pres 5, Res 5.5 (breathes with wide dynamics, classic rock rhythm feel)
  7. Driven Crunch - Gain 6.5, Vol 2, Pres 5.5, Res 6 (thicker mids, earlier sustain, slightly compressed)
  8. Jaws Crunch - Gain 10, Vol 2, Pres 6, Res 6.5 (pronounced distortion, aggressive bite)
  
 Ultra OD1 mode:
  9. Mayweather OD1 - Gain 1.5, Vol 3, Pres 4.5, Res 5.5 (punchy low end, precise palm-muted rhythm tone)
  10. Silk OD1 - Gain 4, Vol 2.5, Pres 5, Res 6 (smooth, compressed sustain)
  11. Kat Dennings OD1 - Gain 7, Vol 2, Pres 5.5, Res 6.5 (stacked gain, dense texture, rhythm tone)
  12. Unleashed OD1 - Gain 10, Vol 1, Pres 6, Res 7 (aggressive saturation, high-gain tone with power)
  
 Ultra OD2 mode:
  13. T-1000 OD2 - Gain 1, Vol 3, Pres 5, Res 6 (liquid metal, lead sustain, long singing notes)
  14. Samurai OD2 - Gain 4, Vol 2, Pres 6, Res 6.5 (mid-forward, cutting lead)
  15. Pushed OD2 - Gain 7, Vol 1.5, Pres 6.5, Res 7 (maximum expressive sustain without chaos)
  16. Marla Singer OD2 - Gain 10, Vol 1, Pres 7, Res 7.5 (a dense, compressed wall of sound)

1895.desc:
*Update v2* Re-trained, 200-500 epochs each, normalization ready!

My personal sweet spots on my Marshall JCM2000 recently serviced. Include both overdrive stacks, crunch, and clean. For the overdrive stacks, I set gain for an AltRock tone, and dimed. AltRock can be pushed to a metal tone by feeding a drive pedal sim into it.

All my captures are made by dialing the tone in with guitars running through the exact same signal path that the capture is done through, so things *should* sound exactly how I heard them when dialing them in through my control room. The only knob you may need to mess with (if at all) is the Input knob in NAM. 

Please don’t contact me asking about specific voltages, levels, what brand of cables I used, the percentage of humidity in the room at the time of capture, what time the sun set this evening, etc. as it doesn’t matter to my ear so long as the sounds are dialed in consistently between “playthrough” and “capture”. The only thing I will say to consider is to shoot for -10db in your DAW, but depending on guitar you may need to range between -8 and -12.

5769.desc: These captures are of a Marshall JCM2000 TSL-100, a three channel hundred-watt head.  I have included one clean channel capture, several crunch channel captures, and two lead channel captures.  This amp has a "classic rock" gain structure, so throw a tube screamer in front if you want to shred!  Preferred IR used to dial in the settings is included.

45918.desc:
Marshall JMP-1 – OD1 Preamp Capture

Epochs: 1000 | Reamp Box: Countryman 85

⚠️Disclaimer: The online preview player does not accurately represent these captures. For the best tone and true-to-source results, please download the files and use them within the NAM plugin alongside a high-quality impulse response at the correct input gain.

Tap into one of the most iconic rack preamps ever made with the Marshall JMP-1 — captured on the OD1 and OD2 modes for classic British crunch with modern precision.

This profile delivers tight lows, rich midrange bite, and a smooth, controlled top end that made the JMP-1 a studio standard throughout the ’90s and beyond. From classic hard rock to saturated lead tones, this capture offers the unmistakable Marshall voice with the fast response and clarity that only the JMP-1 is known for.

Please Note: This is a preamp-only capture — no cabinet IR is included, giving you full flexibility to pair with your preferred cab sims, IRs, or power amp simulations.

Makes and Models
Marshall JMP-1

6367.desc:
A couple of captures I did to cover the more metal side of my Marshall JMP 2203 from 1980. Quite a few captures feature a boost in front of the amp, mostly an Empress ParaEQ MK II Deluxe, the Fortin Grind or the Boss SD-1. I tried to cop tones from Scott Ian, Metallica, and KillerToneTexas (KTT).

This set only features the standard captures, complex captures can be found on my Tonehunt page.

The complex captures (with a "C" suffix) are closer to the real amp, in case you can't run them, use the standard (with an "S" suffix) captures.

Very high quality 1500 epochs standard captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the V3 Test Signal and the 0.9.0 trainer.

Signal Chain: UA Apollo X8 -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> (Booster Pedals) -> Marshall JMP 2203 -> Suhr Reactive Load

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu

5750.desc:
Here's my JTM-style amp head. This is a '67 EL34 Marshall JTM50 replica with a few tweaks.
Its power transformer is a low voltage model that gives a softer breakup with a little less headroom.
This amp has also a split cathode in V1 so Channel 1 is "High Treble" input like later lead plexi models and Channel 2 is "Normal" input.

The two channels were individually captured and connected together with a jumper cable (signal into Ch1 top input, jumping Ch1 bottom input to Ch2 top input).

No extreme EQ settings as I wanted to make this set fit for most guitars (single coil pickups as well as humbuckers).

In this pack, you'll find lot of great British tones from nice clean, edge of breakup, crunch to thick overdrive.

Naming convention on the models follows the knob settings (Volume1/Volume2/Treble/Middle/Bass/Presence).

Amp only but you can find here some Celestion IRs or other Marshall 4x12 IRs to go with it.

Great results with Greenback, G12H30 or V30 speakers.

Technical information:
Interface: Motu M2
Reamp Box: Radial Pro-RMP
Reamp level: 12 dBu
Load Box: DIY Tube-Town reactive load
Captures normalized to: -3 dB
Epochs per capture: 600
ESR on Standard mode: 0.00012 to 0.0062

If you like my work, you can supporting me via paypal.
https://paypal.me/kosmicaudio

67945.desc:
This pack is captures of the Marshall JVM 410H, capturing all four channels across three gain stages each (low, mid, and high). The EQ settings are adjusted specifically for each gain level rather than using a single fixed setting across the board.

Every configuration is captured twice: once clean and once with a Maxon 808 boost engaged, giving you a full set of boosted profiles alongside the clean captures.

All the settings are also captured as full-rigs using two Marshall 4x12 cabs (V30 and Greenbacks)

All profiles in the sample pack are DI captures, trained in Standard format using a Torpedo Captor X+ load box and high grade cables throughout.

52034.desc:
Captured with a Behringer UMC204HD
I've made it for testing purposes, but have fun if you like it! :)

Use a 1x10 speaker IR, or what you prefer!

Check my Ramrod 10" speaker IR as well!

Please share your thoughts! :)

2717.desc: If you're a fan of AC/DC and their legendary guitarist Angus Young, you'll love this Neural Amp Modeler capture of his tone from the album Stiff Upper Lip. This capture faithfully reproduces the raw and powerful sound of his Gibson SG plugged into a Marshall stack, with a touch of boost for extra punch and sustain. Whether you want to rock out to classic tracks like "Stiff Upper Lip" and "Safe in New York City" or create your own riffs and solos, this capture will make you feel like you're on stage with the band. Don't miss this opportunity to unleash your inner rockstar with this amazing Neural Amp Modeler capture of Angus Young's tone!

64481.desc:
Teaser Tone from my current release 
40 Captures of the 1987X
(20 CAB+20 DI)

Marshall 1987X Plexi – NAM Capture Pack



Unleash the sound of a true British icon.


This NAM capture pack delivers 40 high-quality captures of the legendary Marshall 1987X, the iconic 50-watt Plexi famous for its explosive dynamics, raw power, and that unmistakable tone players have been chasing for decades.

From crystal-clear cleans to raging Plexi overdrive, this pack captures every sweet spot of one of the greatest 50W Marshalls ever made—touch-responsive, harmonically rich, and alive under your fingers.

What’s Included:

40 total NAM captures

20 Full Amp captures

20 DI captures for maximum flexibility

Extended gain coverage:

Clean

Edge-of-breakup

Classic crunch

Mean overdrive

Pedal-in-front captures, featuring:

Maxon OD808

J. Rockett PXO

ProCo RAT

MXR YJM Overdrive

4 custom impulse responses included:

Greenback

V30

2 additional hand-selected cabinet options

Detailed PDF guide with all amp and pedal settings used for every capture

Designed for authentic feel, massive punch, and studio-ready tone, this pack is perfect for recording, re-amping, and live use.

 If you’re a Marshall fan or a Plexi purist, don’t miss this one. 


All Trained with 1000 Epoches Standart



Signal Chain: RME Babyface Pro FS ; Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box ; Marshall Vintage Modern; Suhr Reactive Load  Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu

1831.desc:
SETTINGS

CHI: P6 B0 M10 T6 VI8 VII0

DESCRIPTION

A highly authentic clone of the legendary MRSH JT100 amp with all era correct components going down right to the correct date codes. 

This is the amp of early Hendrix, Cream, Free, Who, AC/DC - literally all the big acts of the day used the JT100 amps as Marshall quickly became a standard on the big stages of Rock music. 

It has in some regard similar traits to a JT45, but at the same time it is an entirely different beast. It has an attack like no other Marshall - while having a similar bold character, it sounds stronger, tighter and the notes have a "cluck" to them, a nice definition around the attack that the smaller amps don't have and that, while being more direct and harder never sounds harsh or too much "in your face". 

It sounds particularly great with a Strat neck pickup set to a semi-clean style of tone along the lines Hendrix's "clean" tones. With a Strat this is the sound of Monterey and Are You Experienced. Add a fuzz and it becomes a wild boar that is on the edge of uncontrollable, but still very musical.

Add small amount of reverb at the end (15%) to give it more life

60150.desc:
Marshall YJM100 Amp Pack – NAM Capture Pack

Teaser Tone from my newest Release

Experience one of the most inspiring and versatile Plexi-style amplifiers ever created—now fully captured for the NAM platform.

The Marshall YJM100 delivers everything from pristine cleans to full-throttle high-gain fury, all with the unmistakable character of a true classic.

This Capture Pack includes 25 high-quality DI captures, carefully crafted to recreate the full tonal range of this exceptional amp—including the iconic Yngwie-inspired sound. From vintage warmth to modern aggression, every nuance is preserved.

To ensure maximum flexibility and realism, captures were created using the High, Jumpered, and Low input channels, giving you complete control over your tone inside the NAM ecosystem.



Features:

25 DI captures for NAM
Authentic Plexi tones from clean to aggressive high gain
Captures include High, Jumpered, and Low input channels
Pedal-enhanced tones featuring TS808, TS9, JRockett PXO, and internal amp boost
100W and 50W modes captured for dynamic response options
Includes 7 custom IRs to guide and shape your sound
From smooth cleans to roaring lead tones, this pack delivers a complete Plexi experience in a modern, flexible format. A true “desert island” collection for players who demand both character and versatility.

Plug in, dial up, and unleash the fury.

Enjoy.



Signal Chain: RME Babyface  > Lehle P-Split III > Marshall YJM > Suhr Reactive Load

-13dBu

Full Demo of QC and Tonex HERE

24643.desc:
A bunch of captures of a Matchless Clubman 35 head.

- balanced: 'normal' captures
- dark: dark captures
- bright: bright captures
- SSR: captures with a solid state rectifier instead of tube
- couple of power amp only captures

This amp runs pretty bright so controlling treble with additional eq or carefully selected IRs is needed.

46104.desc:
3 different typical settings of a maxon OD808 pedal for modern high gain metal tones. 

All 3 at zero drive, added a +6 volume boost to the signal for each profile (not needed unless you run into problems with not enough signal).

1) Noon tone / noon balance
2) noon tone / 2pm balance
3) 2pm tone / 2pm balance

24637.desc:
In this special pack, I used my Maxon OD-9 with a vintage RC4558P Texas Instruments chip from the 80's. This particular chip has less gain than the JRC4558D used in my previous packs. The other particularity with the RC4558P chip is that you get a little less pronounced mids than with the JRC4558D. This results in a light overdrive without too much coloration.

Concerning the anti-aliasing aspect, I used a dedicated "super input" file for the captures and the xSTD architecture for the training.

The interest of these profiles is potentially to avoid accumulating aliasing problems with the amp.

For these captures, the Drive has been set from 0 to 10 (Max), the Tone to 5 (12 o'clock) and 7 (about 2 o'clock), and the Level to 10 (Max).

The nam files contain metadata for levels calibration.

Technical information:
Interface: Motu M2
Reamp Box: Radial Pro-RMP
Reamp level: 12 dBu
Captures normalized to: -3 dB
Epochs per capture: 1000
ESR on xSTD mode: 0.000057 to 0.000112

If you like my work, you can supporting me via paypal.
https://paypal.me/kosmicaudio

6298.desc:
If you like my work & want to support it, you can do so here:

https://ko-fi.com/2dornam 🙏

Ladies & gentlemen - the Mesa Boogie Badlander Rectifier 100W  (EL34)

Trained with NAM v0.10.0 using the new methodology Steve published: https://neural-amp-modeler.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorials/calibration.html

• SEND level: 22.903 dBu (10.82 V)
• RETURN level: 13.303 dBu

No need to worry about the above values (SEND / RETURN) anymore - just use NAM plugin v0.7.12 (or later) with the "Calibrate Input" feature. 
Key in your input headroom / maximum input gain inside the "Calibrate Input" textbox and the NAM plugin will do all the math to get profiles to behave as if you were plugging into the real gear itself. 

Reamp chain details:
--------------------

• RME Fireface UCX II -> SignalArt reamp box -> Mesa Badlander 100W -> St.Rock React:IR II -> RME Fireface UCX II

Additional details:
-------------------

• 111 profiles (full Amp head, Preamp only & Poweramp only) included at various settings

• Legendary Tones Mr. Scary Mod included

• Mix of my own settings, settings from the Mesa manual as well as from other artists (Mark Morton, Ola), YouTubers (Kyle Bull, Blake Mansfield) and some forum folks (PaisleyWookiee). 

• A spreadsheet / guide to understanding how each profile was dialed & the source of inspiration: 

https://mega.nz/file/GkgiVKLA#B9a9PeMYsqag5CqT2-TpaX4O19_dNggcaDQSwee4U8g

• AMP (full amp head) & POW (poweramp) profiles are direct; you need to use an IR of your choice

• All profiles use the STANDARD architecture - they will run on the Dimehead NAM player

• ESR graphs available here (most are below the established "Great" accuracy threshold; the rest are as close as the STANDARD NAM architecture allows it to be): 

https://mega.nz/folder/voIBkZwY#qe0wLzjOfdWsUbl87ynBKw
	
• All amp settings were reamped twice: through the "Signal Art" as well as the "Lehle P-SPLIT III". 

• The leading "S-" in the profile name indicates the "Signal Art" reamp box was used. The "Lehle P-SPLIT III" profile pack will use "L-"

!!! ATTENTION, NERDS !!! (yes, that's YOU if you've made it this far):
----------------------------------------------------------------------
 
• Use the [CabMatch] impulse responses I included to adjust the frequency response of the profiles to my Mesa 2x12, Mesa 4x12 or both of them hooked up to the Badlander

• You still need a regular speaker IR after these

• These IRs cover all power ratings (20W, 50W and 100W) across both BOLD and VARIAC modes

• To those familiar with the Fractal units, this is somewhat similar to adjusting the "Speaker Impedance Curve" in the AMP block. 

I will release the "Lehle P-SPLIT III" reamped versions of all these profiles soon.

35162.desc:
These are amp captures of the Crunch channel of a Mesa Boogie Badlander 50 1x12 combo. The amp was set on Bold mode for all captures. Speaker simulation (IRs) will be required. The internal speaker is a Celestion Creamback G12M65. Celestion Vintage 30s are typically paired with the Badlander line.

Settings are in the file name in the following format:
(G)ain (T)reble (M)iddle (B)ass (P)resence (M)aster

I approximated the settings from 0-10.

Standard and xSTD architectures are provided. All captures trained at 1000 Epochs.

8 tone settings x 4 gain settings x 2 architectures = 64 captures
8/13/25 - added gain level at 6

Steinberg UR824 Line Out - DIYRE Line2Amp at 11.5dBu
Mesa Boogie Badlander 50 1x12 Combo
Suhr Reactive Load on Badlander Speaker Out
Steinberg UR824 In at 11.5dBu

Input calibration metadata for 11.5dBu was filled in during training.

37731.desc:
These are new Neural Amp Modeler captures of my Mesa Boogie DC-5 combo amp. These my favorite capture I have made of this amp, and I believe it due a combination of different things. I've been trying to improve my captures and one of the biggest differences is that I used a real cab load during capturing vs just using the Suhr reactive load. It's a big difference in feel and smoothness in tone.

The Mesa Boogie DC-5 was my first tube amp back in 2004 I believe, I regret letting it go but I was able to acquire another one 2 years ago which is the one I'm capturing now. I love this amp so much as it kickstarted a journey for me into tube amps. As soon as I heard this amp cranked up, I was sold on tubes amps and acquired many over the years. So, this amp has very special place in my heart for that reason and the main reason I wanted to get another one. 

There are different gain levels, different graphic eq curves, etc. These are all upboosted captures except the 3 SD 805 really high gain ones. Make sure you check out the demo I have made with these captures on the link below. 

Chain Details: 
Epoch: TONE3000 Best Fit
Reamp box: Lehle p-split iii 
Load box: Suhr Reactive Load
Reamp Reference Level: 11.3 dBu
Interface: Audient EVO 16 

Cheers!

Obi-Juan

1686.desc:
So this is it! One of the coolest amps I have captured! I did 21 Captures trying to get the perfect lead tone and the meanest rhythm tone! I think I did it! Try it out your self!

How does it sound? Check this video out!
https://youtu.be/4N0LSLMcsdk

A full (paid) version of this pack is available here:
https://www.newvintageaudio.com/store/mesa-boogie-jp2c-nam-pack-neural-amp-modeler

If you like what I do and want to support me you can head over to my Youtube channel and show some love!

https://youtube.com/@Rohbemusic

If you feel like contributing to my future quest for awesome tones you can always buy me a coffee, it really helps! And is very much appreciated!

https://www.buymeacoffee.com/rohbemusic

Enjoy!

64728.desc:
Ladies & gents - the Mesa Boogie Mark VII (6L6 loaded).
Randall Smith's last gift to the guitar world before leaving Mesa.

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Mesa Boogie Mark VII NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ Summary

The pack features a Mesa Boogie Mark VII 6L6-loaded head captured using a Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight cabinet loading down the amplifier. 

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ Real cab (Mesa 4x12 OS straight) used (no reactive load)
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD (A1) and REVyHI architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack includes:

• 83 x AMP settings (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 42 x POW settings (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 57 x PRE settings
• 40 x OD pedal settings

TOTAL: 1,694 individual NAM files (847 in STD architecture and 847 in the REVyHI architecture)

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles offer 6 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND #1: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58) blended
• BLEND #2: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58 @ +4dB) blended
• BLEND #3: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 @ +4dB & SM58) blended

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Mesa Boogie Mark VII -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight load) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu

Use the "Calibration" feature (video linked) in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp)
• [PRE] = preamp only (no poweramp)
• [POW] = poweramp only (no preamp)
• [OD] = overdrive pedal
• STD = standard quality architecture; works on the NAM Player
• REVyHI = highest quality architecture (by Rickard Gerthsson)
• 90W / 45W / 25W = poweramp section wattage
• CH1 / CH2 / CH3 = the 3 distinct channels of the amplifier
• CLN = Clean mode
• FAT = Fat Clean mode
• CRNCH = Crunch mode
• MKVII = Mark 7 mode
• MKIIB = Mark IIB mode
• MKIIC = Mark IIC mode
• MKIV = Mark IV mode
• EQ@ON / EQ@OFF = graphic EQ enabled / disabled
• TSMINI = Tube Screamer Mini
• MXRM77 = MXR Custom Badass Modified O.D.
• FORTIN14 = Fortin Fourteen
• DWHAMER = PedalPCB Dwarven Hammer
• DFXKoD = DemonFX King of Drive
• DFXKLON = DemonFX Centaur
• DFXDBOOST = DemonFX Dual Boost, Exotic EP mode
• JR.TheJef = J.Rockett Audio Designs The Jeff Archer
• GRIND = Fortin Grind
• ODD = Warm Audio ODD
