6406.desc:
This is a lead channel capture set of a Mesa Boogie Mark IV. All credit goes to my buddy Kody who recorded all the output clips, I merely trained them.

Kody was also kind enough to provide a manual that documents each capture setting graphically. You can download it here:

https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/x3sii36w9damo4keykymb/Mark-IV-Manual-Final.pdf?rlkey=bdwmokhy4etbggko119npy86f&st=tsuh398v&dl=0

Additional capture sets of the Mark IV will follow sometime in 2025.

This set includes standard ("S" suffix) and complex ("C" suffix) captures. Complex captures require more CPU power, but, if you can run them, they're closer to the real tone of the amp than standard captures, though not by a lot.

All captures were trained with 1300 epochs, which provides a great level of accuracy (very low ESR numbers).

Kody used the following recording chain for the Mark IV capture packs:

Focusrite 18i20 3rd Gen Line Out -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> Mesa Boogie Mark IV -> Suhr Reactive Load IR -> Focusrite 18i20 3rd Gen Line In

Signal Chain Calibration: +9.0 dBu

This capture set contains the calibration meta data already and it can be used with the buil-in calibration system of the newest NAM player to automatically set the correct input gain level for your audio interface.

MG = Mid Gain

SOUND SETTINGS:
bal = balanced
scoop = less mids
mids = mid forward

69212.desc:
Update - 6/5/26 - Shortened file names.

I made Amp Head captures of my Mesa Dual Rectifier Multi-Watt head’s Red Modern mode while it was connected to my Mesa Traditional 4x12 with V30s. 

I took 6 settings and trained them with the following loads:
-Suhr Reactive Load (SRL Suffix) to take advantage of my recently upgraded audio path
-Mesa Traditional 4x12  (4FB Suffix) for the ideal load to this amp. Theoretically will perform accurately with Mesa 4x12 V30 IRs

You can easily scroll through loads in the plugin. Consider it an extra tone control.

The 6 settings are as follows:
1 - MV-2 P-2 T-5 M-4 B-2
2 - MV-2 P-3 T-6 M-4 B-3
3 - MV-2 P-3 T-6 M-6 B-3
4 - MV-2 P-4 T-4 M-3 B-4
5 - MV-2 P-5 T-4 M-5 B-3
6 - MV-2 P-6 T-0 M-7 B-3

I took Setting 1 and boosted with a few pedals. SRL and 4FB load versions available as well. The filenames reference the following abbreviations:
TLDX - Wampler Tumnus Deluxe
SD1 - Boss SD-1
OD808 - Maxon OD808
TMB-66, TMB-73 - Keeley Time Machine Boost 1966 and 1973 modes

The gain on the amp was set at 2:00, which should be enough for late 90s/early 2000s rock. Boosting the capture can bring the sounds into metalcore territory.

I had to retrain the Amp Head captures to the A2 format for this pack. Luckily, I still had the reamp files. I experimented with shortening the TTSV10 training file and my wet reamps.

The TTSV10 input file was from The Tone Scientist. The latest iteration is the TTSV11 file, shortened for A2 training, which you can find on the NAM Facebook group.
https://shop.thetonescientist.com/

I am trying to clear my V10 queue to start experimenting with the V11 file.

Full Rig Signal Chain:
RME UCX II Line Out – United Studio Technology Replay Box at 11.5dBu -
Mesa Dual Rectifier Multi-Watt - Suhr Reactive Load Thru -
Mesa Traditional 4x12 Cabinet – Celestion V30 - Shure SM57 - 
CAPI VP28 - RME UCX II Line In at 11.7dBu

The Suhr Reactive Load was used to take a tap of the Full Rig signal chain to make the Amp Head Captures.

I had the A1 versions of the unboosted 4FB loaded captures (6 captures) if anyone has any use for them.

Input/output calibration metadata for 11.5dBu send/11.7dBu return was filled in during TONE3000 training.

52642.desc:
Ladies & gentlemen - the mighty Mesa Boogie Triple Rectifier Multi-Watt (6L6)

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Mesa Boogie Triple Rectifier Multi-Watt (6L6) NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ Summary

This pack features a recent production Mesa Boogie Triple Rectifier Multi-Watt (6L6 loaded) head captured using a Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight cabinet loading down the amplifier. 

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ Real cab (Mesa 4x12 OS straight) used as load (no loadbox)
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD and REVyHI architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack includes includes:

• 64 x AMP profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 46 x POW profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 50 x PRE profiles
• 35 x OD pedal profiles

TOTAL: 1,490 individual NAM files (745 in STD architecture and 745 in the REVyHI architecture)

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles offer 6 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND #1: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58) blended
• BLEND #2: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58 @ +3dB) blended
• BLEND #3: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 @ +3dB & SM58) blended

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Mesa Boogie Triple Rectifier -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight load) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu

Use the new "Calibration" feature (video linked) in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp)
• [PRE] = preamp only (no poweramp)
• [POW] = poweramp only (no preamp)
• [OD] = overdrive pedal
• DIO = diode poweramp rectifier
• TUB = tube poweramp rectifier
• CH1 = Channel 1
• CH2 = Channel 2
• CH3 = Channel 3
• BLD = Bold poweramp tracking
• SPG = Spongy poweramp tracking
• 50 = 50 watts mode
• 150 = 150 watts mode
• CLN = Clean mode (Channel 1)
• PSH = Pushed mode (Channel 1)
• RAW = Raw mode (Channels 2 & 3)
• VNT = Vintage mode (Channels 2 & 3)
• MDN = Modern mode (Channels 2 & 3)
• STD = standard quality architecture; works on the NAM Player
• REVyHI = highest quality architecture (by Rickard Gerthsson)
• TSMINI = Tube Screamer Mini
• MXRM77 = MXR Custom Badass Modified O.D.
• FORTIN14 = Fortin Fourteen
• DWHAMER = PedalPCB Dwarven Hammer
• DFXKoD = DemonFX King of Drive
• DFXKLON = DemonFX Centaur
• DFXDBOOST = DemonFX Dual Boost, Exotic EP mode
• JR.TheJef = J.Rockett Audio Designs The Jeff Archer
• GRIND = Fortin Grind
• ODD = Warm Audio ODD

📒 Spreadsheet with settings & inspiration for all profiles:

https://mega.nz/file/W4oWiIBI#H8rjPrDKIKUGiCHnwKenghTX9cDP7CQ-3oY9JKGqKpA

5905.desc:
This is a poweramp only capture set, you require another neural amp modeler instance to insert preamp simulations in front of it to get the standard preamp/poweramp experience.

This capture pack is based on a Mesa/Boogie 2:90 poweramp with four matched and newly installed TAD Redbase 6L6GCM power tubes.

My previous 2:90 captures were made with the other half of the poweramp that features four matched TAD Redbase 6L6WGC power tubes.
The 6L6WGC power tubes generate a bit more mids and less bottom end than the 6L6GCM tubes.

Very high quality 1200 epochs standard training captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the V3 Test Signal and the 0.8.1 trainer.

Signal Chain: Apollo X8 -> Walrus Audio Canvas Reamp Box -> Mesa/Boogie 2:90 6L6GCM Poweramp -> Suhr Reactive Load

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu

First number denotes the master volume level and the second number denotes the presence setting.

I also included a master volume *13* setting, that was acquired by cranking the output knob on the Walrus Canvas Reamp box, so that the output wasn't the usual 0.5 Volts but 1.23 Volts instead. The poweramps master volume was set to 10 for the *13* captures.

D stands for "Deep Mode" activation (fixed depth boost circuit) and M stands for "Modern Mode", MD captures have both modes engaged.

In order to calibrate the input gain correctly for poweramp captures, you should first adjust the input gain so that it takes care of your own max input gain value.

Once that is established, see what level's your guitar generates by going straight into the Hi-Z input of your audio interface.

That's the level that your preamp capture should deliver optimally so that the poweramp sees the same input gain with which it was trained to begin with.

31556.desc:
These captures are of my MXR Classic 108 Fuzz, as part of my continued attempts to capture fuzzes.

5/26/26 Update - Retrained with new hardware chain and lower Pedal Volume setting.
RME UCX II Line Out - United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu
MXR Classic 108 Fuzz - RME UCX II In at 11.5dBu (11.7dBu + 0.2dB digital makeup gain)

My input and output were configured for 11.5dBu with input calibration metadata entered during training.

I recommend setting the NAM Output mode to Raw or Calibrated if you use the auto input calibration feature.

Settings approximated from 0-10 in the following order:
(V)olume (F)uzz

The pedal has a Buffer switch, which is indicated in the file name if used.

Cranking the Fuzz control is very noisy. I found more acceptable noise levels with moderate settings.

The captures were made with the TTSV10 input file as a recent experiment with Wet-Dry training. 

These were trained with the Standard Architecture at 1000 Epochs.

57603.desc:
I captured my MXR Distortion+ pedal. 

2 Output Levels x 9 Distortion Levels = 18 Captures

I like the Output at 10 (Maxed) when using lower Distortion levels. When I turn up the Distortion, I back off the Output to 8 (3:00).

Steinberg UR824 Line Out - United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu
MXR Distortion+ - Warm Tonebeast DI
Steinberg UR824 In at 11.5dBu

I recommend setting the NAM Output mode to Raw or Calibrated if you use the auto input calibration feature.

Settings approximated 0-10 in the following order:
(O)output (D)distortion

The captures were made with the TTSV10 input file, an experimental training file shared to the NAM community from The Tone Scientist (https://shop.thetonescientist.com/)

These were trained with the Standard Architecture at 1000 Epochs.

68313.desc:
Another Orange Micro Terror amp head capture, recorded using the same setup as my previous capture.

The signal was split using a Millenium DI-33 Active DI Box: one signal was sent directly to the Scarlett Solo as a clean DI reference, while the second signal was routed through the Orange Micro Terror. The amp’s headphone output was then recorded back into the Scarlett Solo.

For this capture, I used a Fender Player Series Telecaster. Three of the captures were recorded with a Boss DS-1 Distortion pedal placed in front of the amp, providing additional gain and a more aggressive character.

As with the previous capture, the Micro Terror's headphone output does not include an integrated cab sim, making it easy to pair the amp with a wide variety of cabinet simulations.

As a future project, I plan to replace the stock 12AX7 preamp tube with a Sovtek 5751 to explore how the lower gain structure affects the amp's character and response.

32981.desc:
Panama Shaman's are another 20-ish take on the 4 voice Fender, AC-30, JCM 800 and Soldano head with EL 84 output and 4 12AX7s. The output of the amp for capture went from speaker out to CH3 of a Behringer G100 phantom power DI to a decade old Steinberg UR44 audio interface also supplying phantom power through XLR. The output of the the UR44 is sent through line out CH 1, with a 1/2 mono to XLR cable going into a fifteen dollar (today) PYLE PDC21 passive DI XLR *out* with maximum 40 db pad.

The 4 ohm load for the head is a Panama 2x12 stereo cabinet in parallel, with both built in LPads turned to zero for silent output. This is not part of the output chain to the computer.

Yes I am running a passive DI backwards. Because it works. The 1/4 inch input of the Pyle PDC21 then goes to the input of the Panama Shaman.

The DAW I'm using is Reaper. 

I have also used this setup to use NAM amps on the computer as an overdrive for this amp. 

There are no effects or overdrives. All settings are 12 o'clock except gain and volume.

55167.desc:
2026-03-01 Update: added many additional captures with more bass cut, treble cut, and clipping settings.  Try Timmy-10-12-09-M-M-M.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
I emailed Paul Cochraine in 2009 about getting a Timmy pedal - that's how they were sold at the time.  He indulged my request to house the electronics in a slightly larger case with the pedal and power connections on the back of the pedal.  In the larger case he put the clipping switch above the knobs.  He also added two extra eq tweaking switches: a mid switch and a bright switch.  As far as I know this was a unique customization.  This collection of captures features several different configurations of those switches.

Clip:
U = sym 2 (quad diodes) - more 'hair'
M = sym 1 (stock dual diodes) - least 'hair'
D = asym (2 + 1 diodes) - most 'hair'

Mid:
U = mid boost
M - stock Timmy
D - more mid boost

Bright:
U = cut 1
M = stock Timmy
D = cut 2

Naming convention for these captures

Knobs are in o'clock values:
09 = low gain
12 = mid gain
03 = high gain

Volume is unity gain at 12 o'clock on this unit, so that is left there for all captures.

Timmy-gain-trebleCut-BassCut-clip-mid-bright

So Timmy-09-07-07-M-M-M is your base light OD setting.  

Use the NAM eq on all the 'M-M-M' models to trim treble and bass in increments.  There are combos of 3 gain settings and the 3 clipping settings set up like that.

There is another set with controls at 10-09-09.  These represent a common setting that I would use to get just a little more apparent drive.  This set cycles through all of the Mid and Bright switch combos.

So: find a capture that is close to what you want and turn on the NAM player gain and eq to slightly tweak the input level for more gain and make minor adjustments to treble and bass.

64607.desc:
The 30th anniversary reissue of the iconic, the legendary Peavey 5150 Block Letter. 
This is the one Peavey paid special attention to.

If you like my work & want to support me, consider grabbing the ✨full✨ Peavey 6505 1992 Original NAM pack over here:

👉 https://ko-fi.com/2dornam/shop 🙏

ℹ️ “MultiLoad” - what’s this about?

The cabinet (or load) a tube amplifier interacts with has a considerable impact on the tone & feel.
For this pack, I used 3 iconic cabinets and reamped all AMP and POW settings with each cab.  

ℹ️ Summary

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ 3 iconic guitar cabs used as loads (no reactive load):
🟣 Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight
🟣 Mesa 2x12 Horizontal Rectifier (standard, not compact)
🟣 Marshall 4x12 1960BV with Marshall G12 Vintage speakers
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD (A1) and REVyHI architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping
✅ Stompbox / OD profiles included

The full pack includes includes:

• 174 x AMP profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 150 x POW profiles (1 x DI and 5 mic-ed up, full-rig flavors for every knob combination used)
• 55 x PRE profiles
• 39 x OD pedal profiles

TOTAL: 4,076 individual NAM files (2,038 in STD A1 architecture and 2,038 in the REVyHI architecture)

✅ Efficiency 

Streamlined for users across platforms (Dimehead, Valeton GP-5 etc), AMP & POW profiles offer 6 flavors:

• DI = raw poweramp <-> cab signal tapped with a DI box, no Microphone 
• SM57 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM57 on the top-left speaker
• SM58 = full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with a Shure SM58 on the top-right speaker
• BLEND #1: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58) blended
• BLEND #2: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 & SM58 @ +4dB) blended
• BLEND #3: full-rig chain, cab mic-ed with 2 mics (Shure SM57 @ +4dB & SM58) blended

⚙️ Reamp chain details

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> Peavey 6505 1992 Original -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight / Mesa 2x12 Horizontal Rectifier / Marshall 4x12 1960BV) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu

Use the "Calibration" feature (video linked) in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

🏷️ Naming convention summary

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp)
• [PRE] = preamp only (no poweramp)
• [POW] = poweramp only (no preamp)
• [OD] = overdrive pedal
• RHYT = Rhythm channel of the amp
• LEAD = Lead channel of the amp
• STD A1 = standard quality architecture; works on the NAM Player
• REVyHI = highest quality architecture (by Rickard Gerthsson)
• TSMINI = Tube Screamer Mini
• MXRM77 = MXR Custom Badass Modified O.D.
• FORTIN14 = Fortin Fourteen
• DWHAMER = PedalPCB Dwarven Hammer
• DFXKoD = DemonFX King of Drive
• DFXKLON = DemonFX Centaur
• DFXDBOOST = DemonFX Dual Boost, Exotic EP mode
• JR.TheJef = J.Rockett Audio Designs The Jeff Archer
• GRIND = Fortin Grind
• ODD = Warm Audio ODD
• BO$$ = Boss GE-7 Equalizer

📒 Spreadsheet with settings & inspiration for all profiles:

https://mega.nz/file/D0wyDT7A#8EZvvCuKYZZbsvka7QBZJgVJCVhpG23_CXL1hfg1lqI

59615.desc:
A Big Muff that doesn't sound like a Big Muff

UPDATE 2026-03-30: redone all captures due to a strange noise/distortion on the decay in some of the old ones.

Clone of the Pete Cornish G-2 based on the schematic traced by Aion FX
It has four D9E diodes with a Vf comprised between 0.21 and 0.23

Contains metadata for levels calibration
Captured using TTSv10 input file to reduce aliasing 
ESR: 0.01 or below

Pedal settings in the filename:
S=Sustain
T=Tone
V=Volume

36 captures that cover the entire range of the Sustain and Tone knobs in 20% steps (positions 0, 2, 4, 8 and 10), Volume instead was on 10 on all captures, since it doesn't affect the pedal's tone you can use the level control on the NAM plugin/hardware loader in place of it.
When using the calibration, consider 0 dB on the output level of the plugin as the max volume setting on the pedal, iow you can turn it down but don't turn it up if you want to keep it accurate to the real pedal.

Little demo at minute 20:47 of the video

60308.desc:
20 captures extracted from the complete premium pack which contains 216 captures covering the full range of the knobs' rotation, in 20% steps.
You can buy it here: https://shop.thetonescientist.com/products/cornish-ss3-premium-captures-pack-nam

Clone of the Pete Cornish SS-3 based on the schematic traced by Aion FX.
The circuit is basically a tweaked/modded version of the MXR Dist+ with an added bass control.

Contains metadata for levels calibration
Captured using TTSv10 input file to reduce aliasing
ESR: 0.0073 or below

Pedal settings in the filename:
S=Sustain
B=Bass
T=Treble
V=Volume

When using the calibration, consider 0 dB on the output level of the plugin as the max volume setting on the pedal, iow you can turn it down but don't turn it up if you want to keep it accurate to the real pedal.

45294.desc:
The one, the only, the Pro Co RAT 2. A staple of rock tones throughout the ages. This is by far the most versatile dirt pedal to exist.

FREE CAT 2 VST | AU - RAT 2 Clone, sounds exactly like the real pedal. Based in DSP with full knob control.

https://stomptones.gumroad.com/l/CAT2

CAT v1 Distortion VST free - Built with these NAM captures. 

https://stomptones.gumroad.com/l/CAT

Full Pedal 49-file capture pack here for $5:
https://stomptones.gumroad.com/l/RAT

The pedal has two meaningful knobs for neural capture: the filter and distortion I have captured the entire pedal with both knobs hitting all positions of min, 0900, 1100, 1200, 1300, 1500, and max, at every combination possible. This means no matter what tone you are looking for on either Guitar or Bass, you should be able to dial it in.

stomptonesdotcom@gmail.com for support

Doom and Shoegaze focused NAM Packs + VST Plugins
stomptones.com

38592.desc:
If you like my work & want to support it, you can do so here: 

https://ko-fi.com/2dornam 🙏

✅ All profiles calibrated
✅ All 3 channels (Clean, Dumble-derived Overdrive, Lead) covered
✅ Real cab (Mesa 4x12 OS straight) used as load (no loadbox)
✅ AMP, PRE & POW profiles for maximum  flexibility
✅ DI and MIC-ed up flavors for each AMP and POW profile
✅ STD and xSTD architectures
✅ Anti-aliasing signal leveraged during reamping
✅ High-fidelity signal chain used during reamping

For what it's worth, the OVERDRIVE channel on this amp delayed the release as it was engineered to match Mark Tremonti’s own (and favorite) DUMBLE amp. 

The pack includes tones inspired from most YouTube reviews where the sound quality was good/inspiring & knob positions were visible.

Use the new "Calibration" feature in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

Reamp chain details:
----------------------------

• RME Fireface UCX II -> Lehle P-SPLIT III -> PRS MT100 (6L6) -> St.Rock React:IR II (Mesa 4x12 OS Straight load) -> RME Fireface UCX II

• SEND level: 18.995 dBu
• RETURN level: 13.195 dBu

Additional details:
-----------------------

A few clarifications on the naming convention:

• [AMP] = full head (pre & poweramp) of the amp head
• [PRE] = profile of just the preamp section; use a poweramp + IR/cab with it
• [POW] = poweramp only profile; use a preamp with it and an IR
• PRS-MT100 LEAD = the amp was using the LEAD channel
• PRS-MT100 OVERDRIVE = the amp was using the OVERDRIVE (Dumble) channel
• PRS-MT100 CLEAN = the amp was using the CLEAN channel
• STD = uses the STANDARD architecture; works on the Dimehead NAM Player
• xSTD = uses a custom architecture (Андрей Полевой) from the NAM FB group

• A guide to understanding how each profile was dialed & the source of inspiration:

https://mega.nz/file/CpIQ3AzS#_IziewB7RgEjzs_vOwaC2pP3sC2P-Cuvn9i_C15VtUM

1825.desc:
This pack contains a variety of pedals set to boost settings, appropriate to tighten up a high gain amp. Many of the pedals are clones but I will list the OG for ease of recognition. Prince of Tone, Klon, TS9, TC Integrated Preamp, Fortin 33, Fortin Grind, HM-2, Metal Zone, TC Electronic Spark, ROD-10 SD1, ROD-10 DS1, EQ Pedal

All are using feather sized models so they are light on the CPU for stacking with other NAM models.

2741.desc:
Suhr Riot - Middle Voice

I could't find anyone who captured this boutique pedal, so I decided to do it by myself.

I've captured 30 settings.
The drive is 10-25-50-75-85-100 and the tone is 0-25-50-75-100
The level is fixed at 50%
The voice switch was set in the middle. 
I didn't capture Drive at 0 because it felt pretty useless to me.

It's my first capture and I'm pretty happy with it. 
I've used a Presonus 26C straight into the Pedal, I didn't have a reamp at hand. 
The captures were good to my ears, maybe with a reamp the ESR could have been better but the profiles sounds good to me.

ESR goes from 0.004 to 0.013 (high gain) 
Trained locally at 500 Epochs with a RTX2060. Trining time was around 15 hours.

Hope you like it!

64590.desc:
Here are captures of my Supro Black Magick 1695TJ combo:
-DI captures using my Suhr Reactive Load
-DI captures using the Jensen P12Q speaker as a load
-Full Rig captures using a Beyerdynamic M201 on the P12Q (no IR needed)

There are two input jacks to the amp:
-Input 1+2 - provides 2 blendable channels with individual Volume 1 and Volume 2 knobs
-Input 2 - the darker channel controlled by Volume 2

All settings are approximated from 0-10.

The amp is provides varying levels of cleans up to about a setting of 7. A Volume of 8 and beyond gets crunchy. I’ve provided the following Volume settings:
Vol.1 -5 Vol. 2-0: clean
Vol.1 -7 Vol. 2-0: broken up clean
Vol.1 -8 Vol. 2-0 : crunch
Vol.1 -10 Vol. 2-0 : cranked channel
Vol.1 -10 Vol. 2-5: more distortion
Vol.1 -10 Vol. 2-8: even more distortion
Vol.1 -10 Vol. 2-10: the most distortion out of the amp
Vol. 2-5: clean normal channel
Vol. 2-8: crunch normal channel
Vol. 2-10: cranked normal channel

The Tone control behaves as expected up to 7. At 8, the sound becomes significantly brighter and loses some bottom end. The DI captures using the P12Q speaker as a load were done at the following tone settings: 5, 7, 8, 10.

The Full Rigs are provided with 2 on-axis mic positions:
-M201a - 3.0” (7.62cm) from cap center
-M201b - 3.5” (8.89cm) from cap center

My chain for making the DI and Full Rig captures using the Jensen P12Q speaker as a load was the following:

Steinberg UR824 Line Out – United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu - Supro Black Magick - Suhr Reactive Load

Suhr Reactive Load Unbalanced Output -
Warm Tonebeast Instrument In - Steinberg UR824 Line In

Suhr Reactive Load Thru -
Jensen P12Q - Beyerdynamic M201TG -
Stam SA73 - Steinberg UR824 Line In

The connection to the Thru output bypassed the Suhr Reactive Load’s internal load.

Input calibration metadata was filled out for those who use it.

Example Setting:
DI Supro 1695TJ In1-2 V1-10 V2-8 T-7 P12Q
-DI capture
-Input 1+2
-Volume 1: 10 (cranked)
-Volume 2: 8  (3:00)
-Tone: 7 (2:00)
-P12Q used as a load for the DI capture
-Add an IR after the capture

Impulse Responses coming shortly.

6166.desc:
If you like my work & want to support it, you can do so here:

https://ko-fi.com/2dornam 🙏

Synergy Bogner Ecstasy preamp into the power section of the Ceriatone King Kong 100W tube head.

Captured with the St. Rock React:IR II reactive load, EQ-matched to the frequency response (speaker impedance) of the power-amp plugged into my Mesa 4x12 Oversized Straight cabinet.

20 direct (IR is required) profiles covering tones from edge-of-breakup to mean. 

Various combinations of the switches used in shooting these (101B and 100 modes included).

Reamp chain details:

• RME Fireface UCX II -> SignalArt reamp box -> Bogner Ecstasy preamp -> Ceriatone King Kong 100W poweramp -> Loadbox -> RME Fireface UCX II

Use the new "Calibration" feature in the NAM plugin to get the most out of these profiles: 

https://www.neuralampmodeler.com/post/neuralampmodelerplugin-v0-7-12-is-released

All profiles have an ESR lower than the established 0.01 threshold so they are very faithful to the profiled gear.
A slightly larger architecture was used to balance increased accuracy with a very minor bump in CPU usage. 

These are direct profiles so feel free to experiment with IRs of your choice.

36764.desc:
High gain
Channel 3
No boost

Signal chain: Focusrite Clarett+ 8 pre --> Radial Pro RMP-1 (modded to remove 10db pad) --> Mesa Dual Rec 3 channel 100w solo head --> Fractal LB-2 X-Load Reactive Load --> Focusrite

41606.desc:
Synergy Dirty Shirley module, blue channel, less gain gain than top red channel, but crancked up
bass-12, mid scoop to 9, treble - 14

54473.desc: Profile of my Synergy IR20 Head loaded with a Soldano SLO module, settings are in photo.  Same set us as the rest of my profiles.  If you would like a custom profile made, please reach out!

54165.desc: 9 captures of Synergy Fortin Killer Kali module. I used different settings on all captures and one is made with J.Loomis settings. I used the Synergy SYN-20IR head and also poweramps of JCM800, EVH 5150 50W Stealth and Diezel Herbert heads for a different sound flavor. Enjoy

29949.desc:
Syn20IR captures with Tone King Imperial MKII module installed. I'll be updating this with more captures overtime. 

Channel 1 is like a Deluxe Reverb but with a more sculpted tone overall (a little more mids, a tighter low end, less harsh highs). 

Channel 2 is like a more general "Tweed" tone. Probably the closest comparison is a 5e3 Tweed Deluxe (which I believe it's based on) but with a lot more range and less fuzziness/wildness--but its not as close as a match as Channel 1 is to a sculpted Deluxe Reverb. It can even get into early Marshall tones when the midbite knob (simply marked as mb here) is pushed

I'll iterate through gain levels as I capture the module and number them 1-9. "HF" refers to the HF switch being up which is a "High Frequency Compensation" switch. Think of it like a high cut that makes the tone darker and smoother--like a reverse bright switch that emphasizes the low-end and mid-range. On the amp and preamp pedal it appears to be the other way around (where HF up boosts high-end) but its basically just the same thing, just the switch and manual description is reversed on the module.

A/B/C indicates the power amp structure used by the Syn20IR
A--More Fender structure. Big bass, less mids, Sparkly high end.
B--More Marshall/Vox structure. Less and looser bass, more mids, chimey high end, slightly more gain.
C--More Modern structure. Tight bass, more low mids, smoother high end and gain.

47292.desc: A compact DIY clone of the legendary Tascam 424 preamp section. Delivers warm, analog character, perfect for guitars, bass, synths.

5099.desc:
There are not enough TC Electronics pedals on here so I present to you my TC Spark Booster, the full size version with the active EQ, gain and 3 mode switch.

Captured here is the Mid Boost Mode with the level at max and the gain from 0 to 10.

The names of each individual capture contain the pedal settings. These are ideal to beef up a clean tone, break up a clean tone add grit and boost to a dirty tone.

Information:-
Reamp Box: Joyo JDI-48
Interface: Universal Audio Volt 2
DAW: Reaper
Models: Feather
Epochs: 150
ESR: 0.000844 to 0.0004103

42103.desc: Tech 21 Steve Harris SH1 pedal - captured the STEVE HARRIS' personal setting from the manual. Used Darkglass Anagram as Interface.

2480.desc:
Captured by George Boukis, trained by me. Posted with permission, of course :)

First of many! I'm starting off with with my favorites...
The peacekeeper is an awesome "transparent" overdrive, lots of clarity and shimmer

More to come...

Keywords: Thorpy, ThorpyFX, Klon, Klone

56147.desc:
I have some captures of this pedal from a year ago that are good.  But they don't capture the glory of playing the actual pedal.  Tried sets of captures using varying input levels this time.  And used a Yesterday Effects pickup simulator set to simulate the electrical output impedance of a humbucker pickup.  Dropping volume on the guitar with these captures will yield nice tones, but still not as good as playing the actual pedal.

You can find further history of this pedal and the captures from last year at https://www.tone3000.com/tones/page-bender-tonebender-mk-ii-6623

HG_MidEq_MidInput is my personal 'sweet spot'.  Roll guitar volume down to about 5-6 and tone to 6-7 for rhythm/crunch.  Then guitar volume back up to 10 for leads/riffs/heaviness.

Basic pedal info:
- AC125 transistors were used
- 3 position toggle with different values of input caps that contour the bass response: mid, lows, lowest
- A charge pump circuit on the power input that converts from modern 'negative ground' to the 'positive ground' that works with the PNP AC125 transistors.  Makes it compatible with today's standard pedal power supplies
- A voltage trimmer that is set to trim down the 9V power a little - this smooths the top end sound out a tad

190.desc:
Captured my favorite Kemper profile of all times. Tone Junkie Empire 7. Please use the provided Impulse responses for the full experience. And if you have a Kemper get the pack :)

HW from Tone Junkie approved sharing this one :)

50670.desc: Tone King Imperial Preamp, IR section bypassed. Reamper - SWA. Knobs at its best position. Different gain levels.

5599.desc:
Here goes the last amp that I have. This is where I get some of my favorite clean tones. I tried to capture as many of my favorite tones as possible, but know that this amp has a lot of knobs, so it's not feasible for me to capture every combination!

These sound great when put in front of the UA Ox Stomp Two Rock cab models.

File name indicates the settings. For example, BMD G5 T5M5B5 O7 P5 means all bright, mid, deep switches are on, gain = 5, treble 5, mid = 5, bass = 5, master = 7, presence = 5. 

BF, Trad, and MS are the gain structures available on the amp. These stand for Blackface, Traditional Two Rock, and Matt Schofield respectively.

1080.desc:
Vox AC15CH. The head version of the classic Vox AC 15! This amp is perfect for edge of breakup tones, and well suited for indie rock. It’s my go-to for cleans. Stock tubes installed.

I've modeled both the normal and top boost circuits, each at clean, edge of breakup (what Vox does best), crunch, and totally overdriven.

Use with an IR of a blue alnico or a greenback for the classic Vox tone.

Engineered and captured by Matt Goings at Killian Studios.

31267.desc:
This capture set is based on the VOX AC30 CH "custom head" version. It's the classic "Pseudo-Class-A" amplifier with a normal and top boost channel. This head version features an fx loop, a master volume and an attenuator. For this pack, I've turned the fx loop off, set the master volume to maximum and disabled the attenuator completely by choosing the "30W" mode. The integrated Tremolo and Reverb effects were also turned completely off. Only the higher gain inputs were used for both the normal and top boost channel. 

The normal channel has only the volume control, while the top boost channel has a volume, treble and bass control. There's also the famous "Hi Cut" control that is typical for VOX desgins, the newer models use the "Tone Cut" description instead, but the functionality is the same.

The first one or two letters of each capture name, after AC30, are channel labels for the normal ("N") and top boost ("TB") channels. The volume setting ("V") follows afterwards along with tone cut ("TC"). If it's the top boost channel, then there's bass ("B") and treble ("T") control settings as well. Finally, the capture names end with a rough verbal description of the tone. 

Since I'm in possession of a really good Dallas Rangemaster Germanium Treble Booster clone, I made some captures with it in front of the normal channel as well, for the Rory Gallagher and Brian May tones.

There are three types of captures in this set:

- Standard denoted with an "S" suffix
- xStandard denoted with an "XS" suffix (more accurate, similar CPU hit as Standard)
- Complex denoted with a "C" suffix (highest accuracy, highest CPU hit)

If your computer can handle it, go for the complex captures, otherwise xSTD is a reasonable alternative to the standard architecture.

Very high quality 1400 epochs complex captures with very low ESR numbers. Made with the TTS input V10 Test Signal and the hyper accuracy training architecture, which guarantees very low aliasing. Standard and xStandard captures were trained on tonezone3000.com with 1000 epochs.

Signal Chain: UA Apollo X8 Line Output -> Lehle P-Split III Reamp Box -> VOX AC30 CH -> Suhr Reactive Load -> UA Apollo X8 Hi-Z Input

Signal Chain Calibration: +12.4 dBu (Calibration Metadata Included)

36541.desc:
The Pork & Pickle from Way Huge. The captures labeled "Pork" are from the OD side of this pedal which is from the Way Huge Pork Loin.  The "Pickle" side is the fuzz side from the Way Huge Russian Pickle.  Russian Big Muff style.  Massive sounding pedal that's very versatile.  I opted to capture each setting with a clean blend with a bit of highs rolled off which sounded good in my setup.  Hopefully it'll sound good in yours as well!

Captured via the fx loop of the Darkglass Anagram.

30809.desc:
Xotic SL Drive Pedal Captures

Xotic Effects based this pedal on the Marshall Super Lead and Super Bass. It's very amp-like and cleans up well with your guitar's volume knob. Definitely has the vibe of the vintage Marshalls heard on countless records. 

Note there are internal dip switches on this pedal that change the characteristics of the "amp". This particular set of captures have all 4 set to "ON". I plan to upload more captures soon with the various dip switch settings. 

I added one capture of this pedal in front of the Tone King Imperial by Neural DSP as a quick test. 

Reamp Box: Joyo JDI-48
Interface: MOTU M4
DAW: Ableton

Please visit www.skepsisrecordings.com if you are into heavier music and physical media! I also plan to offer capture packs on the site in the near future. Thanks for checking it out!

68604.desc:
I’ve made captures of my Vexter Series Fuzz Factory. 

I dialed in some Stylophone sounding tones with the Comp control high (5 and up). I unfortunately didn’t crack the code for accurately capturing the more traditional Fuzz Face-type tones, so I did not include those attempts.

For the training, I used a pickup emulator based on the AMZ FX project. Although the capture results were turning out harsher than I expected, I enjoyed the 8-bit tones enough to share.

Settings are approximated from 0-10 in the following order:
(V)olume (G)ate (C)omp (D)rive (S)tab

For each Gate/Comp/Drive combination, I set the Stab control in 5 positions. I kept the Drive control midway to avoid the signature oscillations of the Fuzz Factory.

RME UCX II Line Out - United Studio Technologies Replay Box at 11.5dBu
Pickup Emulator - Fuzz Factory - RME UCX II In at 11.7dBu

The pickup emulator project is found here:
https://www.muzique.com/lab/pickups.htm

Calibration metadata was entered for all captures. I recommend setting the NAM Output mode to Raw or Calibrated if you use the auto input calibration feature.

The captures were made with the TTSV10 input file, an experimental training file shared to the NAM community from The Tone Scientist (https://shop.thetonescientist.com/)

These were trained with the Standard Architecture at 1000 Epochs.

45241.desc:
This IR pack captures the classic tones of Ampeg bass cabinets, providing a wide range of options for bassists, producers, and engineers. It includes both 8×10 and SVT cabinet simulations, recorded with multiple speaker models, mic placements, and direct outputs to give you flexible tonal possibilities. These IRs are designed to work with amp simulators, re-amping setups, or direct recording chains.

The Ampeg 8×10 cabinets feature four speaker models: 57, 4033, D6, and e602. Each speaker has two versions labeled A107 and AH, which likely correspond to different mic placements or tonal captures. A107 captures a more on-axis, direct tone, while AH provides an alternative perspective that is more open and ambient, giving you options for both focused and spacious bass sounds.

The Ampeg SVT cabinets are captured with a variety of microphones, including Beta52, Bright Beta52, Bright Neumann, and SM57 off-axis. The different mics offer unique tonal characteristics, from warm and punchy to bright and detailed. There are also direct output IRs, including a standard DI and a “hot” DI version with boosted EQ or level, letting you use the cabinet tone or a pure DI signal as needed.

Overall, this IR pack delivers a complete set of Ampeg cabinet simulations, allowing you to mix and match speaker types, mic placements, and DI options to find the perfect bass tone. Whether you need the growl of an 8×10, the punch of an SVT cab, or a clean DI signal for processing, this pack gives you professional-quality, flexible tones for any project.

Non-commercial IRs found on an old hard drive, I had a lot of fun with them whiile dabbling with my very first modeler on a beautiful summer day (Sonicake Matribox Mk1).

39748.desc:
Created some IRs (96 Khz) with my Eminence Karnivore 8 Ohm Speaker inside of a Engl E412 Pro cab.
I took a white noise sample and amplified it with a Seymour Duncan PowerStage 170 (Solid State Power Amp) and recorded it with three mics (Beyerdynamic M160, Shure SM57, Sennheiser MD 421 Kompakt)

40738.desc:
I made impulse responses of my EVH 5150III 4x12 cabinet loaded with Celestion G12-EVH speakers. I shot all four speakers and have uploaded on-axis IRs in 0.25inch (~0.6cm) increments from 1.00” to 2.5” from the cap. Off-axis IRs were captured in 0.5” increments. I thought that the cap center to 0.75” positions were a little too piercing, but can upload if any wants. 

A decent starting point would be “G12-EVH LL 5150III 4x12 SM57 1.75in 0.0in CL7603.wav”

This cabinet is a Marshall 1960B-style 4x12 cabinet loaded with 16Ohm G12-EVH speakers. I have read that these speakers were relabeled versions of the now-discontinued G12M67 Heritage Greenback. The most obvious pairing would be with an EVH 5150III series head or capture. I think the IRs complement Plexi circuits with big bright caps or JCM800-type amps as well. I've made Full Rig captures with my Mesa Dual Rectifier Multi-Watt and Peavey 6505 on their highest gain channels with this cab for fun.

I shot each microphone position through Chameleon Labs 7603 (Neve 1073-inspired) and CAPI VP28 (API-based) microphone preamps for some variety.
24Bit 48kHz - 500ms - MPT Format

Naming Convention
G12-EVH <speaker position> 5150III 4x12 <mic> <Distance from Center> <Distance Off Grill> <Off Axis Angle, if applicable> <Microphone Preamp>

Speaker Positions are LL – lower left, LR – lower right, UL – upper left, UR – upper right
Equipment Used:
-Shure SM57
-Behringer A800 Power Amp
-Chameleon Labs 7603 Microphone Preamp
-CAPI VP28 Microphone Preamp
-Steinberg UR824 Audio Interface
-Voxengo Deconvolver

10sec sine sweeps were used to excite speaker

60274.desc:
IRs made from the speakers of my 1970 Fender Bassman 2x15 cabinet loaded with the original CTS 8ohm ceramic speakers.

Seven different close mics were used to capture both the upper and lower speakers; R121, SM57, MD421, RE20, SM7B, Beta52, andC414, mic'd in the following positions:

-Cap=center of the cap
-Cap Edge=edge of the cap where it meets the cone
-Cone=midpoint of the cone halfway between cap and cone edge
-Cone Edge=edge of the cone right next to the baffle

One distant mic was also used; C414 mic'd at the center of the cabinet at distances of 12" and 24".

Mics were powered by Vintech Audio 73 mic pre with a little EQ boost at 60Hz and 12kHz, and passed through HCL Varis Tube Vari-Mu compressor for color, into RME Fireface converters. 

Captured using 10 second sine wave sweep @24bit/48kHz

April 2026
Nathaniel@DahmanMusic.com
www.DahmanMusic.com

61705.desc:
The modern day Fender Super Reverb Reissue is equipped with Jensen P10R Reissue speakers. For this pack, I’ve chosen to mic the upper right speaker to minimize the low end buildup. 

I made IRs of the speaker from the cap center to about 4.0” (~10.2cm) from the cap. I moved in 0.25” (0.635cm) increments. I omitted the harshest and muddiest sounding positions, but still wanted to provide a wide range of sounds.

I primarily created these impulse responses for use with my Super Reverb, either loaded down or in NAM format. Captures are below.

https://www.tone3000.com/tones/fender-65-super-reverb-reissue-vibrato-channel-61701

The P10R is also the stock speaker in the Fender Pro Junior IV LTD. I really like how the P10R sounds with that amp. The speaker is also found in the modern Tweed Bassman Reissues, so these IRs could be used for more modern Tweed-style sounds.

Below are links to older captures of my Pro Junior IV.
https://www.tone3000.com/tones/fender-pro-junior-iv-ltd-115dbu-and-cab-irs-4702

The P10R has a mixed reputation, but I think I was able to dial in some decent sounds with my Super Reverb.

I suggest checking out maestrodimusica’s IR pack of his Super Reverb with CTS Alnicos for a more vintage take on the Super Reverb.

My starting point is with the SM57 2.5” away from the cap. I go for any of the other mics when I want a softer attack.

24Bit 48kHz - 500ms - MPT Format
 
Naming Convention
P10R UR 4x10 <Mic Model> <Distance from Center> <Distance Off Grill> <Off Axis Angle, if applicable>

-BU87IC - Beesneez BU87I C Large Diaphragm Condensor
-D1000E - AKG D1000E
-LR100 - Monoprice LR100 Ribbon
-M160 - Beyerdynamic M160 Ribbon
-M201 - Beyerdynamic M201TG
-MK012 - Oktava MK-012 Small Diaphragm Condensor
-OR57 - Shure SM57 with Crimson Orange Handle
-R80 - MXL R80 Ribbon
-R2MII - Golden Age R2 MkII Ribbon
-SM57 - Shure SM57
-ST170 - Sterling ST170 Ribbon

Equipment Used:
-Behringer A800 Power Amp
-Stam SA73 Microphone Preamp
-RME UCX II Audio Interface
-Voxengo Deconvolver

33532.desc:
Loaded with a pair of JBL D120s.

EQ'd for different purposes, from clean articulate rhythm work with no mud, to mid heavy leads.

27221.desc:
Just yet another small collection of IRs, which are all phase aligned to each other. As usual using my Marshall 1960AV, This time using the V7X and SM57 in new-found sweet-spots, together with an RB500 to give a bit more thump. My favorite is the "BlendOfAll", which are all mics equally blended in volume-wise, but feel free to tinker around!

I should make a whole pack at some point in time... ;-)

45022.desc:
I made impulse responses of my Marshall 1960TV 4x12 cabinet loaded with Celestion G12M25 Greenback speakers. I used a Shure SM57 on all four speakers and have uploaded on-axis IRs in 0.25inch (~0.6cm) increments from 1.00” to 2.5” from the cap. Off-axis IRs were captured in 0.5” increments.

A decent starting point would be “M25 UL 1960TV 4x12 SM57 1.75in 0.0in SA73.wav”

The speakers in this cabinet are the newer UK Greenbacks. Both the speakers and the cab have 2020 production dates. I read that the newer UK Greenbacks have a little less top end than the MIC Greenbacks, and it sounds that way to me.

The upper speakers in the slant position sound quite a bit different than the bottom speakers.

I will be using this cab as a load to train my mid-gain amps for both Full Rigs and DI captures. I also intend to provide a Part 2 to this set, but with non-SM57 microphones.

More about the cabinet here:
https://outmodedelectronics.blogspot.com/2025/10/marshall-1960tv-4x12-cabinet.html?m=1

I shot each microphone position through Chameleon Labs 7603 (Neve 1073-inspired), Stam SA-73 (Neve-inspired), and CAPI VP28 (API-based) microphone preamps for some variety.

24Bit 48kHz - 500ms - MPT Format

Naming Convention
M25 <speaker position> 1960TV 4x12 <mic> <Distance from Center> <Distance Off Grill> <Off Axis Angle, if applicable> <Microphone Preamp>

Speaker Positions are LL – lower left, LR – lower right, UL – upper left, UR – upper right

Equipment Used:
-Shure SM57
-Behringer A800 Power Amp
-Chameleon Labs 7603 Microphone Preamp
-Stam SA-73 Microphone Preamp
-CAPI VP28 Microphone Preamp
-Steinberg UR824 Audio Interface
-Voxengo Deconvolver

10sec sine sweeps were used to excite speaker

44078.desc:
This is a blend of two V30 IR's from 4x12 Mesa Rectifier Cabs.   
It's quite possibly the heaviest IR ever made!

45023.desc:
I made impulse responses of my Mesa Boogie 4FB Traditional Straight 4x12 cabinet loaded with Celestion Vintage 30 speakers. I used a Shure SM57 on all four speakers and have uploaded on-axis IRs in 0.25inch (~0.6cm) increments from 0.00” to 2.00” from the cap. Off-axis IRs were captured in 0.5” increments.

5/6/26 - I deleted the 2.25in and 2.50in positions since they are most likely too dark for most users playing rock and metal. See Linked Tones for SM57 IRs backed off the cab to reduce congestion in darker positions.

Recommended starting points vary depending on amp:
Mesa Rectifier Modern Mode - 0.50” from cap
Peavey 5150/6505 Lead - 0.75” from cap

The Upper Left and Lower Left speakers are not as thick sounding.

I will be using this cab as a load to train my high gain amps for both Full Rigs and DI captures with the ideal cab load. I also intend to provide a Part 2 to this set, but with non-SM57 microphones.

The cabinet has a production date of 2002 with 8 ohm Vintage 30s from 2001. The cabinet is comparable in dimensions vs other 4x12 cabinets. It is not as tall as the Mesa Standard Oversized 4x12, which is well represented on TONE3000. These V30 IRs in general are warmer sounding compared to my IRs of my 16 Ohm V30 in various cabs.

More about the cab here:
https://outmodedelectronics.blogspot.com/2025/11/mesa-boogie-rectifier-standard-4x12.html?m=1

I shot each microphone position through Chameleon Labs 7603 (Neve 1073-inspired), Stam SA-73 (Neve-inspired), and CAPI VP28 (API-based) microphone preamps for some variety.

24Bit 48kHz - 500ms - MPT Format

Naming Convention
V30 <speaker position> 4FB 4x12 SM57 <Distance from Center> <Distance Off Grill> <Off Axis Angle, if applicable> <Microphone Preamp>

Speaker Positions are LL – lower left, LR – lower right, UL – upper left, UR – upper right

Equipment Used:
-Shure SM57
-Behringer A800 Power Amp
-Chameleon Labs 7603 Microphone Preamp
-Stam SA-73 Microphone Preamp
-CAPI VP28 Microphone Preamp
-Steinberg UR824 Audio Interface
-Voxengo Deconvolver

10sec sine sweeps were used to excite speaker

29354.desc:
Orange 2x12 Cabinet loaded with Celestion Vintage 30 speakers
Captured with a Sennheiser e906, a Shure SM57, an AKG C 414 and a t.bone RM 700
